1	\chapter{Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence for $\ModuleCat k$-valued sheaf cohomology}
2	\label{chap:Cohomology_MayerVietoris}
3	
4	This chapter extracts (iter-027 split) the Mayer-Vietoris LES infrastructure for $\Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k)$-valued cohomology, mirroring Mathlib's \texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean} and \texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean} line-by-line for the $\ModuleCat\,k$ flavor. The carrier definitions $\HModule$, $\HModulePrime$, etc., live in Chapter~\ref{chap:Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK} and are imported.
5	
6	The build-out is the cohort iter-016 $\to$ iter-026: functorial wrappers (iter-016), LES building blocks $\toBiprod$ and $\fromBiprod$ (iter-017), the composition-is-zero lemma (iter-018), the short-complex carrier and pushout-of-free-sheaves analog (iter-019), the short-exact infrastructure (iter-020), the connecting hom $\delta$ (iter-021), the LES sequence as a $\ComposableArrows$ (iter-022), the technical heart (4 auxiliary lemmas + comparison iso to $\Ext.\contravariantSequence$, iter-023), and the exactness theorem with $\delta$-zero $\mathtt{simp}$ companions (iter-026).
7	
8	\section{Functorial wrappers: cohomology of an open as a presheaf (iter-016)}
9	\label{sec:hmodule_prime_cohomologyPresheaf}
10	
11	After Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_HModule_prime} and Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_zero_linearEquiv} the cohomology-of-an-open carrier $\HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,X$ is well-defined together with its degree-zero $H^0$ identification with a Hom group. The next natural step is the \emph{functoriality of the carrier in the open variable $X$}: assembling $X \mapsto \HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,X$ into a presheaf
12	\[
13	  \HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheaf}\,k\,F\,n \;\colon\; \Cop \;\longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat,
14	\]
15	together with the bifunctorial form (varying $F$ as well)
16	\[
17	  \HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheafFunctor}\,k\,J\,n \;\colon\; \Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k) \;\Longrightarrow\; (\Cop \;\Longrightarrow\; \AddCommGrpCat).
18	\]
19	This mirrors Mathlib's $\Sheaf.\mathtt{cohomologyPresheafFunctor}$ and $\Sheaf.\mathtt{cohomologyPresheaf}$ (\texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean} L90--101) for the $\ModuleCat\,k$ flavor.
20	
21	\subsection{Mathlib gap and codomain}
22	
23	A Mathlib survey shows that Mathlib's $\Sheaf.\mathtt{cohomologyPresheafFunctor}$ has body
24	\[
25	  \Functor.\mathtt{flip}\bigl((\Functor.\mathtt{op}\,(\yoneda \circ (\Functor.\whiskeringRight\,\_\,\_\,\_).\mathrm{obj}\,\AddCommGrpCat.\mathtt{free} \circ \mathtt{presheafToSheaf}\,\_\,\_)) \circ \mathtt{extFunctor}\,n\bigr)
26	\]
27	and codomain $\Sheaf\,J\,\AddCommGrpCat \Longrightarrow (\Cop \Longrightarrow \AddCommGrpCat)$. There is no $\ModuleCat\,k$ flavor of this in Mathlib. Mirroring the body verbatim with $\AddCommGrpCat.\mathtt{free} \rightarrow \ModuleCat.\mathtt{free}\,k$ and starting from $\Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat\,k)$-valued sheaves yields
28	\[
29	  \HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheafFunctor}\,k\,J\,n \;:=\; \Functor.\mathtt{flip}\bigl(((\yoneda \circ (\Functor.\whiskeringRight\,\_\,\_\,\_).\mathrm{obj}\,(\ModuleCat.\mathtt{free}\,k) \circ \mathtt{presheafToSheaf}\,J\,\_).\mathtt{op}) \circ \mathtt{extFunctor}\,n\bigr).
30	\]
31	The \emph{codomain remains $\AddCommGrpCat$}, not $\ModuleCat\,k$, because Mathlib's $\Abelian.\mathtt{extFunctor}\,n \,\colon\, \Cop \Longrightarrow C \Longrightarrow \AddCommGrpCat$ always lands in $\AddCommGrpCat$, regardless of which $\Linear k$-enriched abelian category we start in. (A genuinely $\ModuleCat\,k$-valued analog would require a new $\mathtt{linearExtFunctor}$ that Mathlib does not have; we do not introduce it here.) The per-fiber $\Module\,k$ structure on the evaluation $(\HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheaf}\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,X)$ is preserved through the iter-014 $\HModulePrime$ reducibility: the underlying $\Type\,u$ matches $\HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,X$ definitionally (probe-confirmed \lstinline{rfl}), and that carries the $\Module\,k$ inherited from $\Abelian.\Ext.\mathtt{instModule}$ via the $\Linear\,k$ enrichment.
32	
33	\subsection{Why this is the natural iter-016 target}
34	
35	The Mayer-Vietoris LES theorem (Mathlib \texttt{MayerVietoris.lean}) constructs maps of the form $(F.\mathtt{cohomologyPresheaf}\,n).\mathrm{map}\,S.f_{ij}.\mathtt{op}$ — restriction maps on cohomology induced by morphisms of opens. A $\ModuleCat\,k$-flavored LES therefore needs the analogous contravariant functoriality of $\HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,X$ in $X$. Iter-016 lands this functoriality as the smallest probe-confirmable single-iteration unit; iter-017+ then writes the LES theorem directly, reusing $\HModulePrime\_\mathtt{cohomologyPresheaf}\,k\,F\,n$ for the maps.

94	\label{sec:hmodule_prime_mayer_vietoris_first}
95	
96	After Definitions~\ref{def:Scheme_HModule_prime}--\ref{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_cohomologyPresheaf} the cohomology-of-an-open carrier $\HModulePrime\,k\,F\,n\,X$, its $H^0$ identification, and its contravariant-in-$X$ functoriality (assembled into the presheaf $\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n$) are all in scope. Iter-017 attacks the Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence on the $\ModuleCat\,k$ side directly, mirroring Mathlib's \texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean} (file authored by J.~Riou) line-by-line for $\Sheaf\,J\,\AddCommGrpCat$-valued sheaves but with $F.\cohomologyPresheaf$ replaced by $\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F$. The first two LES building blocks are the sum and difference of two restriction maps; both are one-line term-mode \texttt{biprod.lift} / \texttt{biprod.desc} bodies, probe-confirmed by the iter-017 plan-agent.
97	
98	\subsection{Setup: Mayer-Vietoris squares}
99	
100	A \emph{Mayer-Vietoris square} for a Grothendieck topology $J$ on a category $C$ is, informally, a pullback square in $C$
101	\[
102	  \begin{tikzcd}
103	    X_1 \ar[r, "f_{1,2}"] \ar[d, "f_{1,3}"'] & X_2 \ar[d, "f_{2,4}"] \\
104	    X_3 \ar[r, "f_{3,4}"'] & X_4
105	  \end{tikzcd}
106	\]
107	that is a $J$-cover (in the sense that the pair $\{f_{2,4}, f_{3,4}\} : X_2 \sqcup X_3 \to X_4$ is a covering family for $J$, and the pullback to $X_1$ is the obvious one). Mathlib's $\mathtt{GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare}$ (file \texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean}) bundles this datum together with its sheaf-theoretic exactness properties; we use it as a black box.
108	
109	For us, the canonical example is a 2-affine open cover $X_4 = U_2 \cup U_3$ of an open $X_4 \subseteq C.\mathrm{left}.\mathrm{toTopCat}$ on a curve $C$, with $U_1 = U_2 \cap U_3$. After iter-017 + iter-018 + iter-019 land the LES theorem, this specialisation will be the engine of the Path-2 Serre-finiteness argument (`iter-020+`).
110	
111	\subsection{First LES building block: the sum of two restriction maps}
112	
113	\begin{definition}[Sum of two restriction maps in MV cohomology, $\HModulePrime$ flavor (iter-017)]
114	  \label{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_toBiprod}
115	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_toBiprod}
116	  \uses{def:Scheme_HModule_prime, def:Scheme_HModule_prime_cohomologyPresheaf}
117	  Let $k$ be a field, $C$ a category with $J : \mathrm{GrothendieckTopology}\,C$, and assume $\HasWeakSheafify\,J\,(\mathrm{Type}\,u)$, $\HasSheafify\,J\,(\ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k)$, and $\HasExt(\Sheaf(J, \ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k))$. Let $S : J.\MayerVietorisSquare$, $F : \Sheaf(J, \ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k)$, and $n : \mathbb N$. Define
118	  \[
119	    \HModulePrime\_\toBiprod\,k\,S\,F\,n \;\colon\;
120	    (\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,S.X_4)
121	    \longrightarrow
122	    (\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,S.X_2)
123	    \boxplus
124	    (\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,S.X_3)
125	  \]
126	  by
127	  \[
128	    \HModulePrime\_\toBiprod\,k\,S\,F\,n \;:=\;
129	      \mathrm{biprod.lift}\bigl((\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{map}\,S.f_{2,4}.\mathtt{op},\;\;(\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{map}\,S.f_{3,4}.\mathtt{op}\bigr).
130	  \]
131	\end{definition}
132	
133	\begin{proof}
134	  \uses{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_cohomologyPresheaf}
135	  Direct mirror of Mathlib's $\mathtt{GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod}$ (\texttt{MayerVietoris.lean} L43--46) with $F.\cohomologyPresheaf \rightarrow \HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F$. The biproduct $\boxplus$ is the standard biproduct in $\AddCommGrpCat$ (Mathlib `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Biproducts.lean`); it is well-defined because $\AddCommGrpCat$ has finite biproducts.
136	  Plan-agent \texttt{lean\_run\_code} probe (iter-017) confirms the body typechecks end-to-end against current Mathlib + post-iter-016 project state, kernel-only axioms (\lstinline{propext}, \lstinline{Classical.choice}, \lstinline{Quot.sound}). The closure body is the one-line term \lstinline{biprod.lift ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op) ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op)}.
137	\end{proof}
138	
139	\subsection{Second LES building block: the difference of two restriction maps}
140	
141	\begin{definition}[Difference of two restriction maps in MV cohomology, $\HModulePrime$ flavor (iter-017)]
142	  \label{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_fromBiprod}
143	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_fromBiprod}
144	  \uses{def:Scheme_HModule_prime, def:Scheme_HModule_prime_cohomologyPresheaf}

167	\begin{remark}[Why these are the iter-017 targets]
168	  The Mayer-Vietoris LES theorem (Mathlib \texttt{MayerVietoris.lean} L120--141) constructs a six-term sequence
169	  \[
170	    \cdots \longrightarrow H^n(S.X_4, F) \xrightarrow{\;\toBiprod\;} H^n(S.X_2, F) \boxplus H^n(S.X_3, F) \xrightarrow{\;\fromBiprod\;} H^n(S.X_1, F) \xrightarrow{\;\delta\;} H^{n+1}(S.X_4, F) \longrightarrow \cdots
171	  \]
172	  whose three building blocks are $\toBiprod$, $\fromBiprod$, and the connecting hom $\delta$. The first two are direct one-line term-mode \texttt{biprod.lift} / \texttt{biprod.desc} bodies on the cohomology presheaf's restriction maps; iter-017 lands them. Iter-018 proves the composition-is-zero lemma $\toBiprod \circ \fromBiprod = 0$ (Mathlib L72--75) — the verbatim Mathlib \lstinline{simp only} proof transfers cleanly because every rewrite step is value-category-agnostic (see Section~\ref{sec:hmodule_prime_mayer_vietoris_compose_zero}). Iter-019/020+ then lands the LES sequence itself, the iso to $\Ext.\mathrm{contravariantSequence}$ (Mathlib L120--141), and the exactness theorem.
173	\end{remark}

175	\begin{remark}[Compatibility with Mathlib's $\AddCommGrpCat$-flavored LES]

The biproduct $\boxplus$ in $\AddCommGrpCat$, the morphism additive structure, and the negation are all the same in both worlds. The only difference is the source of the cohomology presheaf: $F : \Sheaf\,J\,\AddCommGrpCat$ in Mathlib vs.\ $F : \Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k)$ in our setup. In particular, every Mathlib-level proof on $\toBiprod$ / $\fromBiprod$ (e.g.\ the composition-is-zero lemma at L72--75) transports verbatim to our setting once we replace `F.cohomologyPresheaf` with `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F`; iter-018 uses this directly (Section~\ref{sec:hmodule_prime_mayer_vietoris_compose_zero}).

45	        CategoryTheory.forget₂ RingCat.{u} AddCommGrpCat.{u}) :=
46	    Limits.comp_preservesLimits _ _
47	  CategoryTheory.hasSheafCompose_of_preservesLimitsOfSize _

46	`ext; rfl`. -/
47	instance Functor.const_additive {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
48	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] : (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Additive where
49	  map_add := by intros; ext; rfl
50	
51	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): `Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear when
52	`D` is `Linear R`-enriched. The `[CategoryTheory.Linear R D]` qualification is
53	load-bearing — bare `[Linear R D]` would be parsed as `Linear` of a functor. -/
54	instance Functor.const_linear {C : Type u} [Category.{v} C] {D : Type u₂}
55	    [Category.{v₂} D] [Preadditive D] (R : Type*) [Semiring R]
56	    [CategoryTheory.Linear R D] :
57	    (Functor.const C : D ⥤ C ⥤ D).Linear R where
58	  map_smul := by intros; ext; rfl

60	namespace Adjunction
61	
62	variable {C₁ : Type u} {D₁ : Type u₂} [Category.{v} C₁] [Category.{v₂} D₁]
63	    [Preadditive C₁] [Preadditive D₁] {F : C₁ ⥤ D₁} {G : D₁ ⥤ C₁} (adj : F ⊣ G)
64	    (R : Type*) [Semiring R] [CategoryTheory.Linear R C₁] [CategoryTheory.Linear R D₁]
65	
66	include adj
67	
68	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `G : D ⥤ C` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,
69	then `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear. Mirrors Mathlib's `left_adjoint_additive`; the
70	`simp` call closes via unit naturality + the `Linear.smul_comp` / `Linear.comp_smul`
71	default simp lemmas. -/
72	lemma left_adjoint_linear [G.Additive] [G.Linear R] : F.Linear R where
73	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).injective (by
74	    simp [adj.homEquiv_unit])
75	
76	/-- Iter-046 (Mathlib gap-fill): if `F : C ⥤ D` is `R`-linear and `adj : F ⊣ G`,

78	lemma right_adjoint_linear [F.Additive] [F.Linear R] : G.Linear R where
79	  map_smul {X Y} f r := (adj.homEquiv _ _).symm.injective (by
80	    simp [adj.homEquiv_counit])
81

85	`change` is load-bearing: it exposes the underlying `homEquiv` form, allowing
86	`simp [homEquiv_unit]` to close via the same naturality argument as in
87	`left_adjoint_linear`. -/
88	noncomputable def homLinearEquiv [F.Additive] [G.Additive] [G.Linear R]
89	    (X : C₁) (Y : D₁) : (F.obj X ⟶ Y) ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ G.obj Y) :=
90	  haveI : F.Linear R := adj.left_adjoint_linear R
91	  { adj.homAddEquiv X Y with
92	    map_smul' := fun r f => by
93	      change adj.homEquiv _ _ (r • f) = r • adj.homEquiv _ _ f
94	      simp [adj.homEquiv_unit] }

96	end Adjunction

129	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` induced by the
130	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
131	restriction along `U ≤ ⊤`. -/
132	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
133	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
134	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
135	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫
136	    C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op

139	map of `kToSection`. -/
140	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
141	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
142	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
143	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom

146	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
147	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
148	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
149	  rfl

152	natural in `U`. -/
153	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
154	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
155	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
156	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
157	  rfl

160	`algebraMap`. -/
161	lemma algebraMap_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
162	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) (r : k) :
163	    (C.left.presheaf.map f).hom (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) r)
164	      = algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj V) r := by
165	  rw [algebraMap_eq_kToSection, algebraMap_eq_kToSection]
166	  exact congrArg (fun (g : (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ _) => g.hom r)

248	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
249	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) : Type (u+1) :=
250	  Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n

252	/-- Phase A step 6 algebraic bridge (iter-010 scaffold): the $k$-linear
253	identification of `HModule k F 0` with the Hom group from the constant
254	sheaf at `ModuleCat.of k k`. Mathlib provides
255	`CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)` in any
256	`Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to the `Linear k`
257	enrichment of `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (auto-inferable from
258	`HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`), this collapses `HModule k F 0` to a
259	`k`-linear Hom group. The closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`;
260	probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-010 plan-agent). Used downstream to
261	identify `H⁰(C, toModuleKSheaf C)` with `Γ(C, O_C)` viewed as a
262	`k`-module on a connected proper `k`-curve. -/
263	noncomputable def HModule_zero_linearEquiv
264	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
265	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
266	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
267	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
268	    HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k]
269	      ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F) :=
270	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
271	
272	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-013 scaffold): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
273	cohomology of an open `X : C` with values in a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)`.
274	Mirrors Mathlib's `Sheaf.H' F n X = (F.cohomologyPresheaf n).obj (op X)`
275	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Basic.lean` L105) for
276	`AddCommGrpCat`-valued sheaves, with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
277	
278	The codomain is `Type u` (not `ModuleCat.{u} k`): `Abelian.Ext` returns a bare
279	`Type u` carrying `Module k` via `Abelian.Ext.instModule` through the `Linear k`
280	enrichment. The `noncomputable abbrev` form (rather than `def`) is required so
281	instance synthesis sees through the wrapper to find `Module k (HModule' k F n X)`
282	and `AddCommGroup (HModule' k F n X)` — under `def`, `Module.finrank` would fail
283	to typecheck (cf. iter-009 design rationale on `HModule`).
284	
285	This is the prerequisite for the iter-014+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris
286	LES (mirror of `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`).
287	The iter-014+ work will state and prove the LES on a `MayerVietorisSquare`,
288	specialising to a 2-affine cover of a proper `k`-curve in iter-015+. The
289	comparison theorem `cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n ≅ HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) n`
290	for an acyclic cover (the technical heart of Path-2) is also queued for
291	iter-015+. -/
292	noncomputable abbrev HModule' (k : Type u) [Field k]
293	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
294	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
295	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
296	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C) : Type u :=
297	  Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
298	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n
299	
300	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-015): the `H⁰` algebraic bridge for `HModule'`,
301	mirroring iter-010's `HModule_zero_linearEquiv` for the iter-014 `HModule'`. The
302	$k$-linear identification of `HModule' k F 0 X` with the Hom group from the
303	sheafified representable `(presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X)`.
304	Mathlib provides `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀ : Ext X Y 0 ≃ₗ[R] (X ⟶ Y)`
305	in any `Linear R`-enriched abelian category; specialised to `R = k` and to the
306	`Linear k`-enriched abelian category `Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (the `Linear k`
307	enrichment is auto-inferable from `HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)` via Mathlib's
308	`Sheaf.linear`), this collapses `HModule' k F 0 X` to a `k`-linear Hom group. The
309	closure body is `Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀`; probe-confirmed one-liner (iter-015
310	plan-agent). Used downstream as the algebraic prerequisite for Stein-factorization-
311	derived `H⁰(C, O_C) ≃ k` on a connected proper `k`-curve (multi-iteration; queued
312	iter-017+ alongside the Mayer-Vietoris LES of iter-016+). -/
313	noncomputable def HModule'_zero_linearEquiv
314	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
315	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
316	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
317	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
318	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C) :
319	    HModule' k F 0 X ≃ₗ[k]
320	      ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
321	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X) ⟶ F) :=
322	  Abelian.Ext.linearEquiv₀
323	
324	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H⁰ transport companion for `HModule k F 0`. The iter-010
325	H⁰ bridge `HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k] ((constantSheaf J _).obj
326	(ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)` identifies the degree-zero cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom
327	group; Mathlib's `Module.Finite.equiv` then transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across.
328	The `.symm` is required: iter-010's bridge has `HModule k F 0` on the LHS and the

330	`HModule` conclusion on the RHS, so we apply `.symm` first. Mirrors iter-037's
331	`module_finite_HModule_of_HModule'_X₄` pattern at degree $0$, with no Mayer-Vietoris
332	machinery required. Used downstream as a building block for `Module.Finite k
333	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` once the Hom-from-constant-sheaf finiteness
334	input is supplied for proper geometrically integral $k$-curves. -/

336	    (k : Type u) [Field k]

339	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))

341	    Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0) :=
342	  Module.Finite.equiv (HModule_zero_linearEquiv k F).symm

344	/-- Iter-038 `Module.Finite` H⁰ transport companion for `HModule' k F 0 X`. Parallel
345	of `Scheme.module_finite_HModule_zero` for the iter-014 sheaf-parameterised carrier

348	degree-zero open-evaluation cohomology with a `k`-linear Hom group from the sheafified

353	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
354	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
355	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
356	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
357	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (X : C)
358	    [Module.Finite k ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj

363	/-- Iter-039 curve specialisation of iter-038's `module_finite_HModule_zero` to
364	the structure sheaf `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` of a `Spec k`-scheme `C`. The

383	/-- Iter-039 curve specialisation of iter-038's `module_finite_HModule'_zero` to

396	    (k : Type u) [Field k]

399	    [Module.Finite k
400	      ((presheafToSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
401	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj U) ⟶
402	          Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :

406	/-- Iter-040 affine cohomology vanishing carrier predicate. Packages the
407	geometric statement that for every affine open `U` of `C.left.toTopCat` and
408	every degree `i > 0`, the open-evaluation cohomology `Scheme.HModule' k F i U`
409	is the zero `k`-module (formulated as `Subsingleton`, since `HModule'` returns
410	a `Type u` rather than a `ModuleCat` object — see iter-014 abbrev). The class
411	is the affine-vanishing input the cover-evaluation chain consumes once the
412	producer instance is supplied (queued for iter-041+; multi-iteration
413	project-local construction expected since Mathlib does not yet provide
414	scheme-level Serre vanishing on affines for the `ModuleCat k`-flavour). -/
415	class IsAffineHModuleVanishing
416	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
417	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
418	    Prop where
419	  subsingleton_HModule' : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat},
420	    AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U → ∀ i, 0 < i →
421	      Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U)
422	
423	/-- Iter-040 immediate consumer of `IsAffineHModuleVanishing`: given the
424	class hypothesis, the open-evaluation cohomology `HModule' k F i U` is
425	`Module.Finite k` for any affine open `U` and any `i > 0`. The proof unfolds
426	the class field to `Subsingleton (HModule' k F i U)` and then invokes
427	Mathlib's auto-derived `Subsingleton M → Module.Finite R M` instance (any
428	subsingleton module is finitely generated by the empty set, hence finite).
429	This consumer is the building block for the cover-evaluation chain: combined
430	with iter-039's `H^0` curve specialisations and iter-037's general-degree
431	corner-bridge transport, it closes the algebraic side of `Module.Finite k
432	(HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 1)` once the producer instance for
433	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing` is supplied. -/
434	theorem module_finite_HModule'_of_isAffineHModuleVanishing
435	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
436	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))

439	    (hU : AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U) (i : ℕ) (hi : 0 < i) :
440	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
441	  have : Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) :=
442	    IsAffineHModuleVanishing.subsingleton_HModule' (F := F) hU i hi
443	  inferInstance
444	
445	/-- Iter-041 H⁰ Hom finiteness carrier predicate. Packages the algebraic
446	statement that for every affine open `U` of `C.left.toTopCat`, the
447	morphism group `((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ free k).obj U) ⟶ F)`
448	is `Module.Finite k`. Morally, $\Gamma(U, F)$ is a finite $k$-module on
449	each affine corner. The class is the H⁰-side parallel of iter-040's
450	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing` and packages the H⁰-side input the cover-
451	evaluation chain consumes once a producer instance is supplied (queued
452	for iter-042+; multi-iteration project-local construction expected since
453	Mathlib does not yet provide scheme-level affine Hom-finiteness for the
454	`ModuleCat k`-flavour). -/

456	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
457	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
458	    Prop where
459	  module_finite_hom : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat},
460	    AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U →
461	      Module.Finite k ((presheafToSheaf
462	          (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
463	        ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj U) ⟶ F)
464	
465	/-- Iter-041 immediate consumer of `IsAffineHModuleHomFinite`: given the
466	class hypothesis, the open-evaluation cohomology `HModule' k F 0 U` is

469	chains through iter-038's `Scheme.module_finite_HModule'_zero`, which
470	transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across iter-015's H⁰ algebraic bridge

472	is the H⁰-side parallel of iter-040's `module_finite_HModule'_of_isAffineHModuleVanishing`. -/
473	theorem module_finite_HModule'_zero_of_isAffineHModuleHomFinite
474	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
475	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
476	    [IsAffineHModuleHomFinite k C F]
477	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
478	    (hU : AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U) :
479	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k F 0 U) :=
480	  have : Module.Finite k ((presheafToSheaf
481	        (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
482	      ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj U) ⟶ F) :=
483	    IsAffineHModuleHomFinite.module_finite_hom hU
484	  Scheme.module_finite_HModule'_zero k F U

486	/-- Iter-042 combined affine-corner `Module.Finite` consumer. Folds iter-040's
487	H>0 affine-vanishing consumer and iter-041's H⁰ Hom-finiteness consumer into
488	a uniform statement covering both cases under both class hypotheses. For any
489	affine open `U` of `C.left.toTopCat` and any cohomological degree `i ≥ 0`,
490	the open-evaluation cohomology `HModule' k F i U` is `Module.Finite k`. The
491	proof case-splits on `Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i` and delegates to iter-040 / iter-041
492	respectively. This is the affine-corner finite-length predicate iter-044+ LES
493	finite-length transport consumes uniformly across every degree. -/
494	theorem module_finite_HModule'_of_affine
495	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
496	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
497	    [IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C F]
498	    [IsAffineHModuleHomFinite k C F]
499	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
500	    (hU : AlgebraicGeometry.IsAffineOpen U) (i : ℕ) :
501	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule' k F i U) := by
502	  rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with rfl | hi
503	  · exact module_finite_HModule'_zero_of_isAffineHModuleHomFinite k C F hU
504	  · exact module_finite_HModule'_of_isAffineHModuleVanishing k C F hU i hi

507	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`. Direct dot-notation wrapper that saves call
508	sites in the curve setting from re-typing the sheaf argument. -/
509	theorem module_finite_HModule'_of_affine_curve
510	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))

512	    [IsAffineHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)]
513	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}

516	  Scheme.module_finite_HModule'_of_affine k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) hU i
517	
518	/-- Iter-043 wholespace H⁰ Hom-finiteness carrier predicate. The producible
519	correction of iter-041's `IsAffineHModuleHomFinite`. Packages the algebraic
520	statement that the global Hom group `((constantSheaf _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)`
521	is finite over `k`. Morally `Γ(C.left, F)` (the **global** sections of `F`)
522	being finite over `k`. On a proper geometrically integral $k$-curve $C$ with
523	$F = O_C$, this is $\Gamma(C, O_C) \simeq k$ (Stein factorization on a proper
524	geometrically connected curve), so this class admits a producer instance
525	from the geometric content of $C$.
526	
527	**Distinction from iter-041's `IsAffineHModuleHomFinite`.** Iter-041's carrier
528	quantifies the Hom-finiteness of `((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ free k).obj U) ⟶ F)`
529	over **all** affine opens `U`. By the Yoneda + free-functor + sheafify
530	adjunctions, this Hom group is `≃ₗ[k] Γ(U, F)`. On a proper smooth $k$-curve
531	$C$ with $F = O_C$, $\Gamma(U, O_C)$ is **NOT finite over $k$** for $U$ a
532	proper affine open — e.g. for the standard cover of $\mathbb{P}^1_k$ by
533	$U_0 = U_1 = \mathbb{A}^1_k$, $\Gamma(U_i, O_{\mathbb{P}^1}) = k[t]$ is
534	infinite over $k$. The iter-041 class therefore admits no producer instance
535	on a non-trivial proper curve — dead scaffolding.
536	
537	The wholespace version here captures only the **global** Hom group, which on
538	a proper curve is finite via Stein. Iter-044+ LES finite-length transport
539	uses this wholespace class (in conjunction with iter-040's H>0 affine
540	vanishing) instead of iter-041's. -/
541	class IsHModuleHomFinite
542	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
543	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) : Prop where
544	  module_finite_hom : Module.Finite k
545	    ((constantSheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat)
546	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k) ⟶ F)
547	
548	/-- Iter-043 immediate consumer: with the wholespace H⁰ Hom-finiteness class
549	hypothesis in scope, transport via iter-038's `module_finite_HModule_zero`
550	(which transports `Module.Finite k`-ness across iter-010's H⁰ algebraic bridge
551	`HModule_zero_linearEquiv : HModule k F 0 ≃ₗ[k] ((constantSheaf _).obj _ ⟶ F)`)
552	to obtain `Module.Finite k (HModule k F 0)`. Parallel to iter-041's consumer
553	but at the global level (one Hom-finiteness instance, not one per affine open). -/
554	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite
555	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
556	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
557	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C F] :
558	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k F 0) :=
559	  have := IsHModuleHomFinite.module_finite_hom (k := k) (C := C) (F := F)
560	  module_finite_HModule_zero k F
561	
562	/-- Iter-043 curve specialisation: direct dot-notation wrapper for
563	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`. Saves call sites in the curve setting from
564	re-typing the sheaf argument when chaining into the LES finite-length
565	transport (queued for iter-044+). Mirrors the iter-039/iter-042 `_curve`
566	patterns. -/
567	theorem module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve
568	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
569	    [IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)] :
570	    Module.Finite k (Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 0) :=
571	  module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite k C _
572	
573	/-- Iter-044 geometric Stein input for the iter-043 wholespace H⁰
574	Hom-finiteness carrier. For `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))` an
575	integral $k$-scheme with proper structure morphism, the global sections
576	$\Gamma(C, O_C)$ form a finite-dimensional $k$-vector space.
577	
578	This is Stein's classical statement, packaged via Mathlib's
579	`AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed`
580	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Morphisms/Proper.lean`):
581	for $X$ integral and $f \colon X \to \Spec K$ universally closed and
582	locally of finite type, the structure-morphism ring map `f.appTop`
583	is module-finite. `IsProper f` packages both `UniversallyClosed f` and
584	`LocallyOfFiniteType f`.
585	
586	The bridge from `RingHom.Finite (C.hom.appTop.hom)` to
587	`Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))` (where the algebra
588	structure on `Γ(C, ⊤)` is iter-006's `kToSection`-derived) uses
589	`RingHom.finite_algebraMap` plus `Module.Finite.of_equiv_equiv` to
590	transport the base ring from intermediate `Γ(Spec k, ⊤)` to `k` along
591	the ring iso `Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)`. The compatibility
592	of algebra maps reduces to showing
593	`kToSection C (op ⊤).hom = (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp (Scheme.ΓSpecIso _).inv.hom`,
594	which collapses via `Subsingleton.elim` on the `⊤ ⟶ ⊤` hom-set +
595	`Functor.map_id`.
596	
597	Iter-045+ consumes this input to assemble the producer instance
598	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` via lifting Mathlib's
599	`constantSheafΓAdj.homEquiv` to a `LinearEquiv` + identification of
600	`Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)` with the underlying global sections
601	+ transport via `Module.Finite.equiv`. -/
602	theorem module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper
603	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
604	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
605	    Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := by
606	  have hf : (C.hom.appTop.hom).Finite :=
607	    AlgebraicGeometry.finite_appTop_of_universallyClosed k C.hom
608	  letI alg2 : Algebra ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
609	               (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
610	    := RingHom.toAlgebra C.hom.appTop.hom
611	  have hM_inter :
612	      Module.Finite ((Spec (CommRingCat.of k)).presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
613	        (C.left.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) := by
614	    rw [← RingHom.finite_algebraMap]; exact hf
615	  refine Module.Finite.of_equiv_equiv
616	    (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv
617	    (RingEquiv.refl _) ?_
618	  ext x
619	  simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, RingEquiv.coe_toRingHom, RingEquiv.refl_apply,
620	    Function.comp_apply, RingHom.algebraMap_toAlgebra]
621	  have h_kts : (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C (Opposite.op ⊤)).hom =
622	                (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom) := by
623	    ext y
624	    simp only [Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection, CommRingCat.hom_comp,
625	      RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
626	    exact congrFun (congrArg (·.hom) (C.left.presheaf.map_id (Opposite.op (⊤ :
627	                TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)))) _
628	  calc (CommRingCat.Hom.hom (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf.kToSection C (Opposite.op ⊤)))
629	        ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv x)
630	       = (C.hom.appTop.hom).comp ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv.hom)
631	          ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).commRingCatIsoToRingEquiv x) :=
632	              congrFun (congrArg DFunLike.coe h_kts) _
633	    _  = C.hom.appTop.hom x := by
634	        simp only [RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply]
635	        congr 1
636	        change ((Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).hom ≫
637	              (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv).hom x = x
638	        rw [Iso.hom_inv_id]; rfl
639	
640	/-- Iter-045: LinearEquiv between the global-sections module
641	`(Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F` (an object of `ModuleCat k`) and the underlying carrier
642	of `F.obj.obj (op ⊤)` for any sheaf `F` on a topological space `X`.
643	
644	The underlying iso comes from `Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections` (Mathlib
645	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/GlobalSections.lean`): on a site with terminal
646	`T`, the global-sections functor is naturally iso to evaluation at `T`. For
647	the topology of opens `Opens.grothendieckTopology X`, the terminal in
648	`TopologicalSpace.Opens X` is the top open `⊤` (this is `Preorder.isTerminalTop`
649	for any preorder with a top element). The categorical iso in `ModuleCat k` is
650	converted to a `LinearEquiv` via `Iso.toLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's standard
651	upgrading of `ModuleCat`-isos to LinearEquivs).
652	
653	Iter-046+ uses this `LinearEquiv` together with the linearised constant-sheaf
654	/ global-sections adjunction (multi-iteration; project-local lift of
655	Mathlib's `Adjunction.homAddEquiv` to `≃ₗ[k]`) to construct the producer
656	instance `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. -/
657	noncomputable def SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top
658	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {X : TopCat.{u}}
659	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
660	    (Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj F
661	      ≃ₗ[k] F.obj.obj (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens X)) :=
662	  ((Sheaf.ΓNatIsoSheafSections (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
663	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) (T := ⊤) (Preorder.isTerminalTop _)).app F).toLinearEquiv
664	
665	/-- Iter-045 immediate consumer: combining iter-044's geometric Stein input
666	`module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper` with `SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top`
667	yields `Module.Finite k ((Sheaf.Γ).obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` for a proper
668	integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`.
669	
670	The `haveI` is necessary because `Module.Finite.equiv` does not
671	auto-synthesise the `[Module.Finite k]` hypothesis on the source: the iter-044
672	declaration's conclusion is `Module.Finite k (C.left.presheaf.obj (op ⊤))`,
673	but the source of `(SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top _ _).symm` is
674	`(toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj (op ⊤)` — these are *the same Module* (via the
675	iter-006 `toModuleKPresheaf_obj` simp lemma) but Lean needs the `haveI` to
676	register the typeclass under the new spelling.
677	
678	This is the algebraic input for the iter-046+ producer-instance assembly:
679	bridging from `Sheaf.Γ.obj` to the Hom group `((constantSheaf @ unit) ⟶ -)`
680	requires the linearised constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction, which is multi-iteration
681	project-local infrastructure. -/
682	theorem module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper
683	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
684	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
685	    Module.Finite k
686	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
687	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)) := by
688	  haveI : Module.Finite k
689	      ((Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C).obj.obj
690	        (Opposite.op (⊤ : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)) : ModuleCat k) :=
691	    module_finite_globalSections_of_isProper k C
692	  exact Module.Finite.equiv
693	    (SheafGammaObj_linearEquiv_top k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)).symm
694	
695	/-- Iter-046: applied LinearEquiv from the constant-sheaf-Γ adjunction. For any
696	sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` and `X : ModuleCat k`, gives a `k`-LinearEquiv
697	between the Hom group `((constantSheaf).obj X ⟶ F)` (sheaf morphisms from the
698	constant sheaf at `X`) and `(X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ).obj F)` (module morphisms into the
699	global sections of `F`).
700	
701	Built from `(constantSheafΓAdj).homLinearEquiv` (iter-046 Mathlib gap-fill).
702	The five `haveI` lines establish the typeclass scaffolding required to invoke
703	the gap-fill: `presheafToSheaf` Linear (via `sheafificationAdjunction`),
704	`constantSheaf` Additive + Linear (via composition), and `Sheaf.Γ` Additive +
705	Linear (via the `right_adjoint_*` propagators along `constantSheafΓAdj`).
706	
707	Used in iter-046's producer instance `instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf`
708	to bridge from `Sheaf.Γ.obj`-finiteness (iter-045) to Hom-from-constantSheaf-
709	finiteness (the `IsHModuleHomFinite` carrier). -/
710	noncomputable def constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv
711	    (k : Type u) [Field k] {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C]
712	    (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
713	    [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)] [HasGlobalSectionsFunctor J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
714	    (X : ModuleCat.{u} k) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :
715	    ((constantSheaf J _).obj X ⟶ F) ≃ₗ[k] (X ⟶ (Sheaf.Γ J _).obj F) :=
716	  haveI : (presheafToSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
717	    (sheafificationAdjunction J _).left_adjoint_linear k
718	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive := by
719	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
720	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Additive :=
721	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_additive
722	  haveI : (constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k := by
723	    unfold constantSheaf; infer_instance
724	  haveI : (Sheaf.Γ J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).Linear k :=
725	    (constantSheafΓAdj J _).right_adjoint_linear k
726	  (constantSheafΓAdj J _).homLinearEquiv k X F
727	
728	/-- Iter-046: Hom-from-`k` upgrade. The Hom group `(ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M)` for
729	`M : ModuleCat k` is canonically `k`-LinearEquivalent to `M` via `f ↦ f 1`.
730	Direct one-liner combining `ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv` (Mathlib's
731	LinearEquiv-version of the underlying-LinearMap correspondence) with
732	`LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf` (the standard `(R →ₗ[R] M) ≃ₗ[S] M` evaluation). -/
733	noncomputable def homFromOne_linearEquiv (k : Type u) [Field k] (M : ModuleCat.{u} k) :
734	    (ModuleCat.of k k ⟶ M) ≃ₗ[k] M :=
735	  (ModuleCat.homLinearEquiv (M := ModuleCat.of k k) (N := M) (S := k)).trans
736	    (LinearMap.ringLmapEquivSelf k k M)
737	
738	/-- Iter-046: **the producer instance** for `IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
739	on a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme `C`. Closes the four-step chain:
740	
741	  (1) `constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 1) bridges the Hom group
742	      `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)` to `(k ⟶ Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))`.
743	  (2) `homFromOne_linearEquiv` (iter-046 step 3) identifies `(k ⟶ M)` with `M`
744	      as `k`-modules.
745	  (3) Combined LinearEquiv from (1)+(2): `((constantSheaf).obj k ⟶ toModuleKSheaf C)
746	      ≃ₗ[k] Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C)`.
747	  (4) `module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper` (iter-045 step 2) provides
748	      `Module.Finite k (Sheaf.Γ.obj (toModuleKSheaf C))` from `[IsIntegral C.left]
749	      [IsProper C.hom]` (iter-044 geometric Stein input).
750	  (5) Transport via `Module.Finite.equiv (.symm)` of the combined LinearEquiv.
751	
752	Once landed, iter-043's curve consumer `module_finite_HModule_zero_of_isHModuleHomFinite_curve`
753	fires automatically on `Module.Finite k (HModule k (toModuleKSheaf C) 0)` queries,
754	closing the H⁰ side of the genus-finrank Module.Finite ladder.
755	
756	Marked `instance` (not `theorem`) — this is a *producer* of a typeclass instance,
757	to be picked up by typeclass synthesis when the consumer asks for
758	`IsHModuleHomFinite k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`. Hypotheses `[IsIntegral C.left]`,
759	`[IsProper C.hom]` are class arguments propagated via instance synthesis from
760	the use site (where `C` is concretely a proper integral `Spec k`-scheme, e.g.\ a
761	smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve). -/
762	noncomputable instance instIsHModuleHomFinite_toModuleKSheaf
763	    (k : Type u) [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
764	    [IsIntegral C.left] [IsProper C.hom] :
765	    IsHModuleHomFinite k C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) where
766	  module_finite_hom := by
767	    haveI := Scheme.module_finite_gammaObj_of_isProper k C
768	    let LE1 := constantSheafGammaHom_linearEquiv k
769	      (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.of k k)
770	      (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)
771	    let LE2 := homFromOne_linearEquiv k
772	      ((Sheaf.Γ (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
773	        (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
774	    exact Module.Finite.equiv (LE1.trans LE2).symm
775	
776	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

778	namespace AlgebraicGeometry

780	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the Čech cochain complex of
781	the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))`,
782	with respect to an arbitrary indexed family of opens `𝒰 : ι → Opens C.left.toTopCat`.
783	Built from Mathlib's `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor` (file
784	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
785	underlying presheaf of `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` (iter-006). The result is a
786	cochain complex valued in `ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`.
787	
788	The cohomology of this complex is `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` below. The
789	downstream comparison theorem (Čech cohomology = derived-functor cohomology
790	= `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C)` for an acyclic cover) is
791	queued for iter-013+; iter-012 only establishes the Čech-side carrier. -/
792	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain_OC
793	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
794	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
795	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
796	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))
797	
798	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-012 scaffold): the `n`-th Čech cohomology
799	of the structure sheaf for an arbitrary indexed open cover. Defined as the
800	`n`-th homology of the Čech cochain complex `Scheme.cechCochain_OC`. The
801	result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k` and therefore carries a `Module k`
802	structure for free; the iter-013+ comparison theorem will identify it
803	with `Scheme.HModule k (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) n` when the cover is
804	acyclic. -/
805	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology_OC
806	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
807	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
808	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
809	  (Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰).homology n
810	
811	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cochain complex generalising iter-012's
812	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC` to any sheaf of `k`-modules `F`, not just the structure
813	sheaf. Built from the same Mathlib `CategoryTheory.cechComplexFunctor`
814	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/Cech.lean`) applied to the
815	underlying presheaf of `F`. The result is a cochain complex valued in
816	`ModuleCat.{u} k`, indexed by `ℕ`. Iter-012's specialisation
817	`Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰` is recovered by setting `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`
818	(see `Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq` below).
819	
820	This generalisation is the foundational scaffolding the iter-048+ Čech-vs-derived
821	comparison theorem will build on: the comparison map
822	`Scheme.cechCohomology k C F 𝒰 n →ₗ[k] Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ᵢ 𝒰 i)`
823	(queued for iter-048) is naturally parameterised over the sheaf `F`, not just
824	the structure sheaf, so the parameterised carrier is the right level of
825	generality. -/
826	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCochain
827	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
828	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
829	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
830	    CochainComplex (ModuleCat.{u} k) ℕ :=
831	  (cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj ((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj F)
832	
833	/-- Iter-047: parameterised Čech cohomology generalising iter-012's
834	`Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`. The `n`-th cohomology of the parameterised Čech
835	cochain complex. The result lives in `ModuleCat.{u} k`. Iter-012's
836	specialisation is recovered by `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq` below. -/
837	noncomputable def Scheme.cechCohomology
838	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
839	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
840	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
841	    ModuleCat.{u} k :=
842	  (Scheme.cechCochain C F 𝒰).homology n
843	
844	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCochain_OC` is definitionally the
845	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCochain`. The
846	proof is `rfl` since iter-012's body is the same `(cechComplexFunctor 𝒰).obj
847	((sheafToPresheaf _ _).obj (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C))` term. Used by downstream
848	consumers (iter-048+) to switch between the iter-012 structure-sheaf-specific
849	form and the iter-047 parameterised form without semantic loss. -/
850	theorem Scheme.cechCochain_OC_eq
851	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
852	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) :
853	    Scheme.cechCochain_OC C 𝒰 = Scheme.cechCochain C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 :=
854	  rfl

856	/-- Iter-047 bridge: iter-012's `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC` is definitionally the
857	`F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` specialisation of `Scheme.cechCohomology`. -/
858	theorem Scheme.cechCohomology_OC_eq
859	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
860	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) (n : ℕ) :
861	    Scheme.cechCohomology_OC C 𝒰 n =
862	      Scheme.cechCohomology C (Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C) 𝒰 n :=
863	  rfl

865	/-- Iter-048: Čech-side acyclicity carrier predicate. The cover `𝒰` is
866	*Čech-acyclic for the sheaf `F` of `k`-modules* on `C` if positive-degree Čech
867	cohomology vanishes (in the `Subsingleton` sense — `Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n`
868	has type `ModuleCat.{u} k`, but `Subsingleton` on the underlying type is the
869	natural and more chainable form). Mirrors the iter-040 / iter-043 carrier-
870	predicate pattern: a single-field `Prop` class capturing a combinatorial
871	vanishing condition that downstream consumers receive as an instance argument.
872	
873	This is the foundational Čech-side input that the iter-051 producer instance for
874	`IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)` will consume, in conjunction
875	with the iter-049+ Čech-vs-derived comparison theorem and iter-048's consumer. -/
876	class Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover
877	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
878	    (F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))
879	    {ι : Type u} (𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat) : Prop where
880	  subsingleton_cechCohomology :
881	    ∀ (n : ℕ), 0 < n → Subsingleton (Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n)
882	
883	/-- Iter-048: subsingleton transport via Čech acyclicity + comparison.
884	
885	Given the iter-048 carrier hypothesis `[IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]` AND an explicit
886	comparison iso `compIso n : cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k] HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰 i)`
887	(the Čech-vs-derived comparison, queued for iter-049+ to construct as a
888	theorem), conclude that `Subsingleton (HModule' k F n (⨆ 𝒰 i))` for `n ≥ 1`.
889	
890	The `compIso` is taken as an *explicit argument*, not a class field. The
891	comparison itself is a `LinearEquiv` (data), so it cannot be a field of a
892	`Prop`-valued class; more importantly, decoupling the Čech-side combinatorial
893	vanishing (iter-048) from the substantive comparison theorem (iter-049+) lets
894	each step land as a single iteration. Iter-049+ will provide the comparison as
895	a theorem, which iter-048's consumer accepts directly via this argument.
896	
897	The consumer body extracts the class field (the `Subsingleton` on
898	`cechCohomology n`) and transports along `(compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton`. -/
899	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover
900	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
901	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
902	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
903	    [Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover F 𝒰]
904	    (compIso : ∀ (n : ℕ),
905	      Scheme.cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n ≃ₗ[k]
906	        Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i))
907	    (n : ℕ) (hn : 0 < n) :
908	    Subsingleton (Scheme.HModule' k F n (⨆ i, 𝒰 i)) := by
909	  haveI := Scheme.IsCechAcyclicCover.subsingleton_cechCohomology
910	    (F := F) (𝒰 := 𝒰) n hn
911	  exact (compIso n).symm.toEquiv.subsingleton
912	
913	/-- Iter-048: curve specialisation at `F := Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C`.
914	
915	Mirrors the iter-039 / iter-042 / iter-043 `_curve` pattern: a thin dot-notation
916	wrapper that saves call sites in the curve setting (where `F` is the structure
917	sheaf) from re-typing `Scheme.toModuleKSheaf C` whenever the iter-048 consumer
918	is chained through. Used by the iter-051 `IsAffineHModuleVanishing k C (toModuleKSheaf C)`
919	producer instance. -/
920	theorem Scheme.subsingleton_HModule'_supr_of_isCechAcyclicCover_curve
921	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))

97	
98	\subsection{Setup: Mayer-Vietoris squares}
99	
100	A \emph{Mayer-Vietoris square} for a Grothendieck topology $J$ on a category $C$ is, informally, a pullback square in $C$
101	\[
102	  \begin{tikzcd}
103	    X_1 \ar[r, "f_{1,2}"] \ar[d, "f_{1,3}"'] & X_2 \ar[d, "f_{2,4}"] \\
104	    X_3 \ar[r, "f_{3,4}"'] & X_4
105	  \end{tikzcd}
106	\]
107	that is a $J$-cover (in the sense that the pair $\{f_{2,4}, f_{3,4}\} : X_2 \sqcup X_3 \to X_4$ is a covering family for $J$, and the pullback to $X_1$ is the obvious one). Mathlib's $\mathtt{GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare}$ (file \texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean}) bundles this datum together with its sheaf-theoretic exactness properties; we use it as a black box.
108	
109	For us, the canonical example is a 2-affine open cover $X_4 = U_2 \cup U_3$ of an open $X_4 \subseteq C.\mathrm{left}.\mathrm{toTopCat}$ on a curve $C$, with $U_1 = U_2 \cap U_3$. After iter-017 + iter-018 + iter-019 land the LES theorem, this specialisation will be the engine of the Path-2 Serre-finiteness argument (`iter-020+`).
110	
111	\subsection{First LES building block: the sum of two restriction maps}
112	
113	\begin{definition}[Sum of two restriction maps in MV cohomology, $\HModulePrime$ flavor (iter-017)]
114	  \label{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_toBiprod}
115	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_toBiprod}
116	  \uses{def:Scheme_HModule_prime, def:Scheme_HModule_prime_cohomologyPresheaf}
117	  Let $k$ be a field, $C$ a category with $J : \mathrm{GrothendieckTopology}\,C$, and assume $\HasWeakSheafify\,J\,(\mathrm{Type}\,u)$, $\HasSheafify\,J\,(\ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k)$, and $\HasExt(\Sheaf(J, \ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k))$. Let $S : J.\MayerVietorisSquare$, $F : \Sheaf(J, \ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k)$, and $n : \mathbb N$. Define
118	  \[
119	    \HModulePrime\_\toBiprod\,k\,S\,F\,n \;\colon\;
120	    (\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,S.X_4)
121	    \longrightarrow
122	    (\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,S.X_2)
123	    \boxplus
124	    (\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,S.X_3)
125	  \]
126	  by
127	  \[
128	    \HModulePrime\_\toBiprod\,k\,S\,F\,n \;:=\;
129	      \mathrm{biprod.lift}\bigl((\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{map}\,S.f_{2,4}.\mathtt{op},\;\;(\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{map}\,S.f_{3,4}.\mathtt{op}\bigr).
130	  \]
131	\end{definition}
132	
133	\begin{proof}
134	  \uses{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_cohomologyPresheaf}
135	  Direct mirror of Mathlib's $\mathtt{GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod}$ (\texttt{MayerVietoris.lean} L43--46) with $F.\cohomologyPresheaf \rightarrow \HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F$. The biproduct $\boxplus$ is the standard biproduct in $\AddCommGrpCat$ (Mathlib `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Biproducts.lean`); it is well-defined because $\AddCommGrpCat$ has finite biproducts.
136	  Plan-agent \texttt{lean\_run\_code} probe (iter-017) confirms the body typechecks end-to-end against current Mathlib + post-iter-016 project state, kernel-only axioms (\lstinline{propext}, \lstinline{Classical.choice}, \lstinline{Quot.sound}). The closure body is the one-line term \lstinline{biprod.lift ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op) ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op)}.
137	\end{proof}

139	\subsection{Second LES building block: the difference of two restriction maps}
140	
141	\begin{definition}[Difference of two restriction maps in MV cohomology, $\HModulePrime$ flavor (iter-017)]
142	  \label{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_fromBiprod}
143	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_fromBiprod}
144	  \uses{def:Scheme_HModule_prime, def:Scheme_HModule_prime_cohomologyPresheaf}
145	  In the setting of Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_toBiprod}, define
146	  \[
147	    \HModulePrime\_\fromBiprod\,k\,S\,F\,n \;\colon\;
148	    (\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,S.X_2)
149	    \boxplus
150	    (\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,S.X_3)
151	    \longrightarrow
152	    (\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,S.X_1)
153	  \]
154	  by
155	  \[
156	    \HModulePrime\_\fromBiprod\,k\,S\,F\,n \;:=\;
157	      \mathrm{biprod.desc}\bigl((\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{map}\,S.f_{1,2}.\mathtt{op},\;\;-(\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{map}\,S.f_{1,3}.\mathtt{op}\bigr).
158	  \]
159	\end{definition}
160	
161	\begin{proof}
162	  \uses{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_cohomologyPresheaf}
163	  Direct mirror of Mathlib's $\mathtt{GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.fromBiprod}$ (\texttt{MayerVietoris.lean} L67--70) with $F.\cohomologyPresheaf \rightarrow \HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F$. The negation $-(\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{map}\,S.f_{1,3}.\mathtt{op}$ is the additive inverse in the additive structure of $\AddCommGrpCat$-morphisms (the codomain category is $\AddCommGrpCat$ which is preadditive, so morphisms have additive inverses); the sign is the standard convention from the Mayer-Vietoris LES, encoding the alternating-sum structure of the Čech complex.
164	  Plan-agent \texttt{lean\_run\_code} probe (iter-017) confirms the body typechecks end-to-end against current Mathlib + post-iter-016 project state, kernel-only axioms. The closure body is the one-line term \lstinline{biprod.desc ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₂.op) (-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op)}.
165	\end{proof}
166	
167	\begin{remark}[Why these are the iter-017 targets]
168	  The Mayer-Vietoris LES theorem (Mathlib \texttt{MayerVietoris.lean} L120--141) constructs a six-term sequence
169	  \[
170	    \cdots \longrightarrow H^n(S.X_4, F) \xrightarrow{\;\toBiprod\;} H^n(S.X_2, F) \boxplus H^n(S.X_3, F) \xrightarrow{\;\fromBiprod\;} H^n(S.X_1, F) \xrightarrow{\;\delta\;} H^{n+1}(S.X_4, F) \longrightarrow \cdots
171	  \]
172	  whose three building blocks are $\toBiprod$, $\fromBiprod$, and the connecting hom $\delta$. The first two are direct one-line term-mode \texttt{biprod.lift} / \texttt{biprod.desc} bodies on the cohomology presheaf's restriction maps; iter-017 lands them. Iter-018 proves the composition-is-zero lemma $\toBiprod \circ \fromBiprod = 0$ (Mathlib L72--75) — the verbatim Mathlib \lstinline{simp only} proof transfers cleanly because every rewrite step is value-category-agnostic (see Section~\ref{sec:hmodule_prime_mayer_vietoris_compose_zero}). The connecting hom $\delta$ requires the extension class machinery \lstinline{Ext.precomp} (Mathlib \texttt{Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/ExactSequences.lean}) applied to the Mayer-Vietoris square's induced short exact sequence; iter-019+ will scope it because Mathlib's \lstinline{MayerVietorisSquare.shortComplex_shortExact} (Mathlib \texttt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean} L240--268) is built from \lstinline{AddCommGrpCat.free}-yoneda — the $\ModuleCat\,k$ flavour requires a parallel construction with \lstinline{ModuleCat.free k}-yoneda, which is substantive multi-iteration work. Iter-019/020+ then lands the LES sequence itself, the iso to $\Ext.\mathrm{contravariantSequence}$ (Mathlib L120--141), and the exactness theorem.
173	\end{remark}
174	
175	\begin{remark}[Compatibility with Mathlib's $\AddCommGrpCat$-flavored LES]
176	  By construction, the codomain $\AddCommGrpCat$ of $\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf$ matches Mathlib's $F.\cohomologyPresheaf$ codomain (forced by $\Abelian.\extFunctor\,n$ always landing in $\AddCommGrpCat$). The biproduct $\boxplus$ in $\AddCommGrpCat$, the morphism additive structure, and the negation are all the same in both worlds. The only difference is the source of the cohomology presheaf: $F : \Sheaf\,J\,\AddCommGrpCat$ in Mathlib vs.\ $F : \Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k)$ in our setup. In particular, every Mathlib-level proof on $\toBiprod$ / $\fromBiprod$ (e.g.\ the composition-is-zero lemma at L72--75) transports verbatim to our setting once we replace `F.cohomologyPresheaf` with `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F`; iter-018 uses this directly (Section~\ref{sec:hmodule_prime_mayer_vietoris_compose_zero}).
177	\end{remark}

179	\section{Mayer-Vietoris LES building block: composition-is-zero (iter-018)}
180	\label{sec:hmodule_prime_mayer_vietoris_compose_zero}
181	
182	After Definitions~\ref{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_toBiprod}--\ref{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_fromBiprod} the two MV-LES building blocks $\toBiprod$ and $\fromBiprod$ are in scope. Iter-018 proves the third LES building block — the composition-is-zero relation $\toBiprod \circ \fromBiprod = 0$, which is one of the two zero-composition lemmas underpinning the eventual exactness theorem (the other being $\delta \circ \toBiprod = 0$, queued iter-019+).
183	
184	\begin{lemma}[Composition $\toBiprod \circ \fromBiprod$ vanishes (iter-018)]
185	  \label{lem:Scheme_HModule_prime_toBiprod_fromBiprod}
186	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_toBiprod_fromBiprod}
187	  \uses{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_toBiprod, def:Scheme_HModule_prime_fromBiprod}
188	  In the setting of Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_toBiprod}, the composition
189	  \[
190	    \HModulePrime\_\toBiprod\,k\,S\,F\,n \;\circ\; \HModulePrime\_\fromBiprod\,k\,S\,F\,n
191	    \;=\; 0
192	  \]
193	  in the morphism set $(\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,S.X_4) \to (\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{obj}\,(\mathrm{op}\,S.X_1)$.
194	\end{lemma}
195	
196	\begin{proof}
197	  \uses{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_toBiprod, def:Scheme_HModule_prime_fromBiprod}
198	  Direct mirror of Mathlib's $\mathtt{GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod\_fromBiprod}$ (\texttt{MayerVietoris.lean} L72--75). The proof reads as the composition of seven verbatim rewrites in a single \lstinline{simp only} block:
199	  \begin{enumerate}
200	    \item \lstinline{[HModule'_toBiprod, HModule'_fromBiprod]}: unfold the two iter-017 LES building blocks to expose the \lstinline{biprod.lift} / \lstinline{biprod.desc} shape.
201	    \item \lstinline{[biprod.lift_desc]}: apply the biproduct universal property
202	      \[ \mathrm{biprod.lift}\,a\,b \;\circ\; \mathrm{biprod.desc}\,c\,d \;=\; (a \circ c) + (b \circ d). \]
203	    \item \lstinline{[Preadditive.comp_neg]}: push the negation in $\fromBiprod$'s second argument through the composition.
204	    \item \lstinline{[← sub_eq_add_neg]}: rewrite the resulting $a + (-b)$ as $a - b$.
205	    \item \lstinline{[sub_eq_zero]}: reduce $a - b = 0$ to $a = b$ in the ambient \lstinline{AddCommGroup}.
206	    \item \lstinline{[← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp]}: fuse the two consecutive applications of $(\HModulePrime\_\cohomologyPresheaf\,k\,F\,n).\mathrm{map}$ via contravariant functoriality (the $\mathrm{op}$ direction makes the morphism order swap).
207	    \item \lstinline{[S.toSquare.fac]}: close by the Mayer-Vietoris square's pullback-square factorisation $S.f_{1,2} \circ S.f_{2,4} = S.f_{1,3} \circ S.f_{3,4}$.
208	  \end{enumerate}
209	  Every rewrite step is independent of the value category: \lstinline{biprod.lift_desc} holds in any preadditive category with biproducts; \lstinline{Preadditive.comp_neg} holds in any preadditive category; \lstinline{sub_eq_add_neg}/\lstinline{sub_eq_zero} are elementary $\AddCommGroup$ arithmetic; \lstinline{Functor.map_comp}/\lstinline{op_comp} are categorical functoriality on $\Cop$; \lstinline{S.toSquare.fac} is independent of the value category entirely. Hence the proof transfers verbatim from $\Sheaf\,J\,\AddCommGrpCat$ to $\Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat\,k)$ with no modification.
210	
211	  Plan-agent \texttt{lean\_run\_code} probe (iter-018) confirms both the un-attributed \lstinline{lemma} form and the full \lstinline{@[reassoc (attr := simp)]}-attributed form (matching Mathlib L72) typecheck end-to-end against current Mathlib + post-iter-017 project state, kernel-only axioms (\lstinline{propext}, \lstinline{Classical.choice}, \lstinline{Quot.sound}). The \lstinline{@[reassoc (attr := simp)]} attribute is load-bearing: \lstinline{@[reassoc]} generates the post-composition variant $\toBiprod \circ \fromBiprod \circ Z = 0 \circ Z = 0$ for downstream use, and \lstinline{(attr := simp)} makes the lemma a \lstinline{simp}-set member.
212	\end{proof}
213	
214	\begin{remark}[Why $\delta$ is iter-019+ and not iter-018]
215	  Mathlib's connecting hom $\delta$ (\texttt{MayerVietoris.lean} L112--114) reads
216	  \[
217	    \delta \;:=\; \mathtt{AddCommGrpCat.ofHom}\,(S.\mathtt{shortComplex\_shortExact}.\mathtt{extClass}.\mathtt{precomp}\,\_\,(\mathtt{by\ omega}))
218	  \]
219	  using $S.\mathtt{shortComplex\_shortExact}$ — a $\Sheaf\,J\,\AddCommGrpCat$-valued short exact sequence constructed in $\mathtt{Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean}$ L240--268 from $\AddCommGrpCat.\free$-yoneda. Constructing the analogous $S.\mathtt{shortComplex\_shortExact}$ in $\Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat\,k)$ requires:
220	  \begin{enumerate}
221	    \item A parallel \lstinline{shortComplex} built from \lstinline{ModuleCat.free k}-yoneda (mirrors Mathlib L240--250).
222	    \item Mono / Epi instance derivations on the two morphisms (mirror Mathlib L252--257, ~3--5 LOC each).
223	    \item An $\mathtt{isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf}$-analog (mirror Mathlib L259--261; the technical heart, possibly involving a $\mathtt{ModuleCat}$-valued $\mathtt{isPushout}$ argument that may need its own lemma).
224	    \item The exactness derivation \lstinline{shortComplex_exact} via \lstinline{ShortComplex.exact_of_g_is_cokernel} on the analogous cokernel cofork (mirror Mathlib L263--265).
225	    \item The packaging into \lstinline{shortComplex_shortExact}.
226	  \end{enumerate}
227	  Total estimated scope: 15--25 LOC of new infrastructure plus 5--10 LOC of glue. This is genuinely substantive multi-iteration work and would inflate the iter-018 scope beyond the project's standard "one declaration per iteration" cadence. Iter-019+ will scaffold this; iter-020+ will use it to define $\delta$, the LES sequence, and prove exactness.
228	\end{remark}

230	\section{Mayer-Vietoris LES short-complex infrastructure (iter-019)}
231	\label{sec:hmodule_prime_mayer_vietoris_short_complex}
232	
233	Iter-019 lays the carrier of the eventual $\Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat\,k)$-flavored short exact sequence on which the connecting hom $\delta$ of the Mayer-Vietoris LES is built. The short complex is a direct mirror of Mathlib's $\AddCommGrpCat$-flavored \lstinline{MayerVietorisSquare.shortComplex} (\texttt{MayerVietorisSquare.lean} L234--249), with $\AddCommGrpCat.\free$ replaced by $\ModuleCat.\free\,k$. The infrastructure decomposes into three declarations: a one-line Mathlib gap-fill instance, a pushout-of-free-sheaves analog (lemma), and the short-complex carrier (definition).
234	
235	\subsection{Mathlib gap and the helper instance}
236	
237	Mathlib registers $(\AddCommGrpCat.\free).\IsLeftAdjoint$ in \lstinline{Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Adjunctions.lean} L84 as a typeclass instance, enabling $\Sheaf.\composeAndSheafify\,J\,\AddCommGrpCat.\free$ to acquire the necessary $\PreservesColimit$ instance. Mathlib's \lstinline{ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean} provides the adjunction \lstinline{ModuleCat.adj k : ModuleCat.free k ⊣ forget (ModuleCat k)} but does not register the corresponding $\IsLeftAdjoint$ instance (that registration lives only in \lstinline{ModuleCat/Topology/Basic.lean} for topological modules, an unrelated context). This is a real Mathlib gap on the path to a $\ModuleCat\,k$-flavored Mayer-Vietoris LES.
238	
239	The iter-019 helper fills the gap with a one-line instance:
240	
241	\begin{definition}[Helper instance: $\ModuleCat.\free\,k$ is left adjoint (iter-019)]
242	  \label{def:Scheme_ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint}
243	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint}
244	  \uses{}
245	  Let $k : \mathrm{Type}\,u$ be a field. Then $\ModuleCat.\free\,k$ is a left adjoint:
246	  \[
247	    (\ModuleCat.\free\,k).\IsLeftAdjoint \;:=\; \langle \_, \langle \ModuleCat.\mathtt{adj}\,k \rangle\rangle.
248	  \]
249	\end{definition}
250	
251	\begin{proof}
252	  \uses{}
253	  The body is a structural term-mode value packaging Mathlib's existing \lstinline{ModuleCat.adj k : ModuleCat.free k ⊣ forget (ModuleCat k)} into the typeclass-witness form expected by \lstinline{Functor.IsLeftAdjoint}. No proof tactics; just typeclass registration. Plan-agent \texttt{lean\_run\_code} probe (iter-019) confirms the full block typechecks end-to-end against current Mathlib + post-iter-018 project state, kernel-only axioms.
254	\end{proof}
255	
256	\subsection{The pushout-of-free-sheaves analog}
257	
258	\begin{lemma}[Pushout of free $\ModuleCat\,k$-sheaves under a Mayer-Vietoris square (iter-019)]
259	  \label{lem:Scheme_HModule_prime_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf}
260	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf}
261	  \uses{def:Scheme_ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint}
262	  Let $k$ be a field, $C$ a category with $J : \mathrm{GrothendieckTopology}\,C$, and assume $\HasWeakSheafify\,J\,(\mathrm{Type}\,u)$ and $\HasSheafify\,J\,(\ModuleCat.\{u\}\,k)$. Let $S : J.\MayerVietorisSquare$. Then the image of $S$ under the composite
263	  \[
264	    \yoneda \;\circ\; \Functor.\whiskeringRight\,(\ModuleCat.\free\,k) \;\circ\; \presheafToSheaf\,J\,\_
265	  \]
266	  is a pushout square in $\Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat\,k)$.
267	\end{lemma}
268	
269	\begin{proof}
270	  \uses{def:Scheme_ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint}
271	  Direct mirror of Mathlib's $\mathtt{GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.isPushoutAddCommGrpFreeSheaf}$ (\texttt{MayerVietorisSquare.lean} L154--160) with $\AddCommGrpCat.\free \rightarrow \ModuleCat.\free\,k$. The proof reads as: (1) start with $S.\isPushout$, the type-level pushout; (2) push it through $\Sheaf.\composeAndSheafify\,J\,(\ModuleCat.\free\,k)$ (which preserves pushouts because $\ModuleCat.\free\,k$ is a left adjoint, registered by Definition~\ref{def:Scheme_ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint}); (3) translate the resulting square's shape via the canonical iso $\presheafToSheaf\composeAndSheafify\mathrm{Iso}$ between two ways of writing $\presheafToSheaf\,J\,\_ \circ (\yoneda \circ \mathrm{whiskerRight}\,\free)$. Body:
272	  \begin{lstlisting}
273	  (S.isPushout.map (Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k))).of_iso
274	    ((Square.mapFunctor.mapIso
275	      (presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso J (ModuleCat.free k))).app
276	        (S.map yoneda))
277	  \end{lstlisting}
278	  Plan-agent \texttt{lean\_run\_code} probe (iter-019) confirms the body typechecks end-to-end against current Mathlib + post-iter-018 project state, kernel-only axioms (\lstinline{propext}, \lstinline{Classical.choice}, \lstinline{Quot.sound}). Every component (\lstinline{Sheaf.composeAndSheafify}, \lstinline{Square.IsPushout.map}, \lstinline{Square.IsPushout.of_iso}, \lstinline{presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso}, \lstinline{Square.mapFunctor.mapIso}) is value-category-agnostic in Mathlib; the proof transfers cleanly.
279	\end{proof}

281	\subsection{The short-complex carrier}
282	
283	\begin{definition}[Short complex of free $\ModuleCat\,k$-sheaves (iter-019)]
284	  \label{def:Scheme_HModule_prime_shortComplex}
285	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_shortComplex}
286	  \uses{def:Scheme_ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint, lem:Scheme_HModule_prime_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf}
287	  In the setting of Lemma~\ref{lem:Scheme_HModule_prime_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf}, define the short complex of $\Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat\,k)$:
288	  \[
289	    (\presheafToSheaf\,J\,\_)\,(\yoneda\,S.X_1 \circ \ModuleCat.\free\,k)
290	    \xrightarrow{\;f\;}
291	    (\presheafToSheaf\,J\,\_)\,(\yoneda\,S.X_2 \circ \ModuleCat.\free\,k) \boxplus (\presheafToSheaf\,J\,\_)\,(\yoneda\,S.X_3 \circ \ModuleCat.\free\,k)
292	    \xrightarrow{\;g\;}
293	    (\presheafToSheaf\,J\,\_)\,(\yoneda\,S.X_4 \circ \ModuleCat.\free\,k)
294	  \]
295	  where $f$ is the biproduct lift of the pair $(\presheafToSheaf\,(\mathrm{whiskerRight}\,(\yoneda.\mathrm{map}\,S.f_{1,2})), -\presheafToSheaf\,(\mathrm{whiskerRight}\,(\yoneda.\mathrm{map}\,S.f_{1,3})))$ and $g$ is the biproduct desc of the pair $(\presheafToSheaf\,(\mathrm{whiskerRight}\,(\yoneda.\mathrm{map}\,S.f_{2,4})), \presheafToSheaf\,(\mathrm{whiskerRight}\,(\yoneda.\mathrm{map}\,S.f_{3,4})))$. The composition-zero relation $f \circ g = 0$ follows from the cokernel-cofork condition of the pushout in Lemma~\ref{lem:Scheme_HModule_prime_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf}.
296	\end{definition}
297	
298	\begin{proof}
299	  \uses{lem:Scheme_HModule_prime_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf}
300	  Direct mirror of Mathlib's $\mathtt{GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.shortComplex}$ (\texttt{MayerVietorisSquare.lean} L234--249) for the $\ModuleCat\,k$ flavor with $\AddCommGrpCat.\free \rightarrow \ModuleCat.\free\,k$. The five $\mathrm{Type}$-valued fields ($X_1, X_2, X_3, f, g$) are filled inline as term-mode constructions; the $\Prop$-valued $\mathtt{zero}$ field follows from the cokernel-cofork condition of the pushout square. Body for the $\mathtt{zero}$ field:
301	  \begin{lstlisting}
302	  (S.map (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
303	      presheafToSheaf J _)).cokernelCofork.condition
304	  \end{lstlisting}
305	  The \lstinline{@[simps]} attribute generates field-projection simp lemmas (\lstinline{HModule'_shortComplex_X₁}, \lstinline{..._X₂}, \lstinline{..._X₃}, \lstinline{..._f}, \lstinline{..._g}) consumed by the iter-020+ $\mathtt{Mono}/\mathtt{Epi}/\mathtt{Exact}/\mathtt{ShortExact}$ instance proofs and the iter-022+ $\delta$ definition. Plan-agent \texttt{lean\_run\_code} probe (iter-019) confirms the body typechecks end-to-end, kernel-only axioms.
306	\end{proof}
307	
308	\begin{remark}[Why this is the natural iter-019 step]
309	  Mathlib's connecting hom $\delta$ for the Mayer-Vietoris LES (\texttt{MayerVietoris.lean} L112--114) reads
310	  \[
311	    \delta \;:=\; \mathtt{AddCommGrpCat.ofHom}\,(S.\mathtt{shortComplex\_shortExact}.\mathtt{extClass}.\mathtt{precomp}\,\_\,(\mathtt{by\ omega})),
312	  \]
313	  which uses $S.\mathtt{shortComplex\_shortExact}$ — a $\Sheaf\,J\,\AddCommGrpCat$-valued short exact sequence (Mathlib L267--268). To obtain a $\ModuleCat\,k$-flavored $\delta$, we need the analog $S.\HModulePrime\_\mathtt{shortComplex\_shortExact}$ in $\Sheaf\,J\,(\ModuleCat\,k)$. This decomposes naturally into:
314	  \begin{enumerate}
315	    \item \textbf{Iter-019:} the carrier $\HModulePrime\_\shortComplex$ + the underlying pushout lemma + the helper instance (this section).
316	    \item \textbf{Iter-020+:} $\mathtt{Mono}\,\HModulePrime\_\shortComplex.f$, $\mathtt{Epi}\,\HModulePrime\_\shortComplex.g$, $\HModulePrime\_\shortComplex.\mathtt{Exact}$ instance derivations (mirror Mathlib L252--265, ~10--15 LOC).
317	    \item \textbf{Iter-021:} the named lemma $\HModulePrime\_\shortComplex\_\mathtt{shortExact}$ packaging the above into a $\mathtt{ShortExact}$ structure (mirror Mathlib L267--268, ~3 LOC).
318	    \item \textbf{Iter-022+:} the connecting hom $\HModulePrime\_\delta$ via $\mathtt{ModuleCat.ofHom}\,(\mathtt{shortComplex\_shortExact}.\mathtt{extClass}.\mathtt{precomp})$.
319	    \item \textbf{Iter-024+:} the LES sequence $\HModulePrime\_\mathtt{sequence}$ ($\mathtt{ComposableArrows}$ form), the iso $\HModulePrime\_\mathtt{sequenceIso}$ to $\Ext.\mathtt{contravariantSequence}$, and the exactness theorem $\HModulePrime\_\mathtt{sequence\_exact}$ (substantive; mirrors Mathlib L120--141).
