35	/-! ## Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill: `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` as a `LinearEquiv`
36	
37	Mathlib's `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ Ext.{w'} X Y n`
38	(`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540) is a bare
39	`Equiv` only. The iter-034 universe-bridge `HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv`
40	(below in `section CoverTotality`) requires it as a `LinearEquiv` over the
41	`Linear R C`-enriched abelian category. The upgrade is mechanical: the
42	`AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` and `Module R (Ext X Y n)` instances are themselves
43	transferred from the standard derived category through `homEquiv`, and
44	Mathlib's load-bearing intermediate lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv` (Basic.lean
45	L543–545) shows that `chgUniv` preserves the underlying derived-category
46	morphism. So `chgUniv` automatically commutes with `+` and `•`. Two short
47	helper lemmas (`chgUniv_add` and `chgUniv_smul`) plus a 7-line `LinearEquiv`
48	structure literal give the upgrade.
49	
50	This cohort lives outside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme` because the
51	helpers belong to the `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext` namespace. We use the
52	qualified-name pattern `Abelian.Ext.foo` rather than wrapping the entire file
53	in a second namespace block. -/
54	
55	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (a): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is additive. The
56	underlying `homEquiv` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean`
57	L162) is preserved by `chgUniv` (Mathlib lemma `Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv`,
58	L543–545), and the `AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n)` instance (L241–243) is defined by
59	transferring the `AddCommGroup` from the standard derived category through
60	`homEquiv`. Combining these via the `Ext.ext` extensionality (L177–179) gives
61	additivity. -/
62	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_add
63	    {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C]
64	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ℕ} (a b : Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n) :
65	    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b) =
66	      Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a + Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b := by
67	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
68	  ext
69	  rw [Abelian.Ext.add_hom, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply,
70	      ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply]
71	  change Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (a + b)) =
72	    Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a) +
73	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} b)
74	  rw [Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv,
75	      Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, ← Abelian.Ext.add_hom]
76	
77	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (b): `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` is `R`-linear when `C`
78	is `Linear R`-enriched. Same proof shape as `chgUniv_add`, but using
79	`smul_hom` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Linear.lean` L52–55)
80	in place of `add_hom`. -/
81	private lemma Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_smul
82	    {R : Type*} [Ring R] {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [Linear R C]
83	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ℕ}
84	    (r : R) (a : Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n) :
85	    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (r • a) = r • Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a := by
86	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
87	  ext
88	  rw [Abelian.Ext.smul_hom, ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply,
89	      ← Abelian.Ext.homAddEquiv_apply]
90	  change Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} (r • a)) =
91	    r • Abelian.Ext.homEquiv (Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a)
92	  rw [Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv, Abelian.Ext.homEquiv_chgUniv,
93	      ← Abelian.Ext.smul_hom]
94	
95	/-- Iter-034 Mathlib gap-fill (c): the `R`-linear upgrade of Mathlib's bare-`Equiv`
96	`Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ Ext.{w'} X Y n` to a `LinearEquiv`,
97	when `C` is `Linear R`-enriched abelian. Combines (a) and (b). The
98	`Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540 declaration is
99	universe-polymorphic but only proves the underlying bijection; this wrapper
100	adds the algebraic structure preservation needed for downstream `LinearEquiv`
101	chaining (specifically, the iter-034 universe bridge
102	`HModule'_eq_HModule_linearEquiv` below). -/
103	noncomputable def Abelian.Ext.chgUnivLinearEquiv
104	    {R : Type*} [Ring R] {C : Type*} [Category C] [Abelian C] [Linear R C]
105	    [HasExt.{w} C] [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ℕ} :
106	    Abelian.Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ₗ[R] Abelian.Ext.{w'} X Y n where
107	  toFun a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'} a
108	  invFun a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w} a
109	  left_inv a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'}.left_inv a
110	  right_inv a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{w'}.right_inv a
111	  map_add' a b := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_add a b
112	  map_smul' r a := Abelian.Ext.chgUniv_smul r a
113	
114	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
115	
116	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the substantive functor mirroring
117	Mathlib's `Sheaf.cohomologyPresheafFunctor` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Sends
118	a sheaf `F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)` to the presheaf
119	`X ↦ Ext^n((presheafToSheaf J _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj X), F)`,
120	i.e.\ to a presheaf `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is
121	`HModule' k F n X` (definitionally, see `HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf` below).
122	
123	The codomain is `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` (not `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ ModuleCat k`) because Mathlib's
124	`Abelian.extFunctor n : Cᵒᵖ ⥤ C ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` always lands in `AddCommGrpCat`
125	regardless of the source category's `Linear`-enrichment; the per-fiber `Module k`
126	structure on `(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (op X)` is preserved
127	through the iter-014 `HModule'` reducibility chain. Probe-confirmed body
128	(iter-016 plan-agent). Used downstream as the prerequisite for the queued
129	iter-017+ `ModuleCat k`-flavored Mayer-Vietoris LES. -/
130	noncomputable def HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor
131	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
132	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] (J : GrothendieckTopology C)
133	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
134	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
135	    (n : ℕ) :
136	    Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k) ⥤ Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
137	  Functor.flip
138	    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
139	      presheafToSheaf _ _).op ⋙ Abelian.extFunctor n)
140	
141	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-016): the abbrev wrapper mirroring Mathlib's
142	`Sheaf.cohomologyPresheaf` for the `ModuleCat k` flavor. Evaluates the
143	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` at a sheaf `F`, giving a presheaf
144	`Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat` whose value at `op X` is `HModule' k F n X` definitionally
145	(probe-confirmed `rfl` by the iter-016 plan-agent). The `noncomputable abbrev`
146	form is required for the per-fiber definitional identification with `HModule'`;
147	under `def` the wrapper would block the `rfl`-level reduction that downstream
148	Mayer-Vietoris and Stein-factorization arguments rely on. -/
149	noncomputable abbrev HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf
150	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
151	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
152	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
153	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
154	    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
155	    Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat :=
156	  (HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor k J n).obj F
157	
158	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the first Mayer-Vietoris LES building
159	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the sum of the two restriction maps
160	`(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₂₄.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₃₄.op`,
161	as a single map into the biproduct. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
162	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod` (file
163	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L43–46) for
164	the `ModuleCat k` flavor with `F.cohomologyPresheaf → HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F`.
165	
166	The codomain `(... ).obj (op S.X₂) ⊞ (...).obj (op S.X₃)` is the biproduct in
167	`AddCommGrpCat` (Mathlib `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Biproducts.lean`); since
168	`HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n` is `Cᵒᵖ ⥤ AddCommGrpCat`, the biproduct
169	auto-resolves. Probe-confirmed body (iter-017 plan-agent). Used downstream as the
170	first of three Mayer-Vietoris LES building blocks; iter-018 will add the
171	composition-is-zero lemma and the connecting hom `δ`, iter-019 the LES sequence
172	and exactness theorem. -/
173	noncomputable def HModule'_toBiprod
174	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
175	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
176	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
177	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
178	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
179	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) ⟶
180	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
181	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) :=
182	  biprod.lift ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op)
183	              ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op)
184	
185	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-017): the second Mayer-Vietoris LES building
186	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the difference of the two restriction maps
187	`(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₂.op` and `(cohomologyPresheaf F n).map S.f₁₃.op`,
188	as a single map out of the biproduct. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
189	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.fromBiprod` (file
190	`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`, L67–70) for
191	the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
192	
193	The negation `-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op` uses the
194	preadditive structure on `AddCommGrpCat`-morphisms; the sign is the standard
195	Mayer-Vietoris convention encoding the alternating-sum structure of the Čech
196	complex. Probe-confirmed body (iter-017 plan-agent). -/
197	noncomputable def HModule'_fromBiprod
198	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
199	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
200	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
201	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
202	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
203	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
204	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃) ⟶
205	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) :=
206	  biprod.desc ((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₂.op)
207	              (-(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op)
208	
209	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-018): the third Mayer-Vietoris LES building
210	block on the `ModuleCat k` side — the composition-is-zero lemma asserting
211	`HModule'_toBiprod k S F n ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n = 0`. Direct mirror
212	of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.toBiprod_fromBiprod`
213	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`,
214	L72–75) for the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
215	
216	The proof unwinds via the biproduct universal property
217	(`biprod.lift_desc : biprod.lift a b ≫ biprod.desc c d = a ≫ c + b ≫ d`),
218	the preadditive negation (`Preadditive.comp_neg`), the addition-of-negation
219	arithmetic (`← sub_eq_add_neg`), the zero-iff-equal arithmetic
220	(`sub_eq_zero`), the contravariant functoriality of `cohomologyPresheaf`
221	(`← Functor.map_comp`, `← op_comp`), and the Mayer-Vietoris square
222	factorisation `S.toSquare.fac` (which says `S.f₁₂ ≫ S.f₂₄ = S.f₁₃ ≫ S.f₃₄`).
223	Probe-confirmed proof (iter-018 plan-agent); the `simp only` set transfers
224	verbatim from Mathlib's `AddCommGrpCat`-flavored proof because every step
225	is value-category-agnostic.
226	
227	The `@[reassoc (attr := simp)]` attribute generates the post-composition
228	form `… ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod … ≫ Z = 0 ≫ Z = 0` and registers the lemma
229	as a `simp` lemma; both are required for downstream Mayer-Vietoris LES
230	exactness arguments. -/
231	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
232	lemma HModule'_toBiprod_fromBiprod
233	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
234	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
235	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
236	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
237	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) :
238	    HModule'_toBiprod k S F n ≫ HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n = 0 := by
239	  simp only [HModule'_toBiprod, HModule'_fromBiprod, biprod.lift_desc,
240	    Preadditive.comp_neg, ← sub_eq_add_neg, sub_eq_zero,
241	    ← Functor.map_comp, ← op_comp, S.toSquare.fac]
242	
243	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019 helper, Mathlib gap-fill): `ModuleCat.free k`
244	is left adjoint to `forget (ModuleCat k)`. Mathlib's
245	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean` provides the adjunction
246	`ModuleCat.adj k : ModuleCat.free k ⊣ forget (ModuleCat k)` but does not register
247	the corresponding `IsLeftAdjoint` instance (whereas `AddCommGrpCat.free.IsLeftAdjoint`
248	is registered in `Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Adjunctions.lean` L84). This
249	project-local instance fills the gap so that `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k)`
250	acquires the necessary `PreservesColimit` instance, used by
251	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf` below. -/
252	instance ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint
253	    (k : Type u) [Field k] : (ModuleCat.free k).IsLeftAdjoint :=
254	  ⟨_, ⟨ModuleCat.adj k⟩⟩

256	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
257	pushout-of-free-sheaves analog of Mathlib's
258	`GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.isPushoutAddCommGrpFreeSheaf`
259	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean`, L154–160).
260	States that the image of the Mayer-Vietoris square `S` under the composite
261	`yoneda ⋙ Functor.whiskeringRight ⋅ (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙ presheafToSheaf J _`
262	is a pushout square in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. The proof transfers the
263	type-level pushout `S.isPushout` through `Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k)`
264	(which preserves pushouts because `(ModuleCat.free k).IsLeftAdjoint`, registered
265	in `ModuleCat_free_isLeftAdjoint` above) and adjusts via the canonical iso
266	`presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso`.
267	
268	Probe-confirmed body (iter-019 plan-agent); the proof is verbatim from Mathlib L156–160
269	with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. Used downstream in iter-020+ to
270	prove `Mono` / `Epi` / `Exact` instances on the iter-019 short complex
271	`HModule'_shortComplex`, and ultimately in the iter-022+ `δ` connecting hom. -/
272	lemma HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf
273	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
274	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
275	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
276	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
277	    (S.map (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
278	      presheafToSheaf J _)).IsPushout :=
279	  (S.isPushout.map (Sheaf.composeAndSheafify J (ModuleCat.free k))).of_iso
280	    ((Square.mapFunctor.mapIso
281	      (presheafToSheafCompComposeAndSheafifyIso J (ModuleCat.free k))).app
282	        (S.map yoneda))
283	
284	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-019): the `ModuleCat k`-flavored
285	short complex of free sheaves underlying the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
286	Direct mirror of Mathlib's `GrothendieckTopology.MayerVietorisSquare.shortComplex`
287	(file `Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/MayerVietorisSquare.lean`, L234–249) for the
288	`ModuleCat k` flavor with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
289	
290	The objects are the sheafified free `ModuleCat k`-valued presheaves on the four
291	vertices of the Mayer-Vietoris square (with the middle pair `S.X₂` and `S.X₃`
292	biproduct-summed). The two morphisms are: `f` (the difference `(yoneda.map S.f₁₂)
293	– (yoneda.map S.f₁₃)` lifted through the biproduct), and `g` (the sum
294	`(yoneda.map S.f₂₄) + (yoneda.map S.f₃₄)` desced through the biproduct).
295	The `zero` proof (i.e.\ `f ≫ g = 0`) follows from the
296	`cokernelCofork.condition` of the pushout square in
297	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf` above.
298	
299	Probe-confirmed body (iter-019 plan-agent); structure-literal mirror of Mathlib L235–249
300	with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The `@[simps]` attribute generates
301	field-projection simp lemmas (`HModule'_shortComplex_X₁`, `..._X₂`, `..._X₃`,
302	`..._f`, `..._g`) consumed by the iter-020+ `Mono`/`Epi`/`Exact`/`ShortExact`
303	proofs and the iter-022+ `δ` definition. -/
304	@[simps]
305	noncomputable def HModule'_shortComplex
306	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
307	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
308	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
309	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
310	    ShortComplex (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) where
311	  X₁ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₁ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
312	  X₂ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₂ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k) ⊞
313	    (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₃ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
314	  X₃ := (presheafToSheaf J _).obj (yoneda.obj S.X₄ ⋙ ModuleCat.free k)
315	  f :=
316	    biprod.lift
317	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₁₂) _))
318	      (-(presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₁₃) _))
319	  g :=
320	    biprod.desc
321	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₂₄) _))
322	      ((presheafToSheaf J _).map (Functor.whiskerRight (yoneda.map S.f₃₄) _))
323	  zero :=
324	    (S.map (yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k) ⋙
325	        presheafToSheaf J _)).cokernelCofork.condition
326	
327	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020 helper, Mathlib gap-fill): the free-module
328	functor `ModuleCat.free k : Type u ⥤ ModuleCat.{u} k` preserves monomorphisms.
329	Mathlib registers `AddCommGrpCat.instPreservesMonomorphismsFree :
330	AddCommGrpCat.free.PreservesMonomorphisms` in
331	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/Grp/Adjunctions.lean` but does not register the
332	corresponding instance for `ModuleCat.free k` in
333	`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Adjunctions.lean`. This project-local
334	instance fills the gap so that, after `simp only [biprod.lift_snd]`, the
335	typeclass-search engine can discharge the residual `Mono` goal in the proof
336	of `HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono` below.
337	
338	The proof: for an injective function `f : X → Y` between types,
339	`(ModuleCat.free k).map f = ModuleCat.ofHom (Finsupp.lmapDomain k k f)` (defeq),
340	and `Finsupp.mapDomain f` is injective on `Finsupp X k → Finsupp Y k` by
341	Mathlib's `Finsupp.mapDomain_injective`. Bridging via `ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective`
342	recovers `Mono` in `ModuleCat k`. -/
343	instance ModuleCat_free_preservesMonomorphisms
344	    (k : Type u) [Field k] : (ModuleCat.free k).PreservesMonomorphisms := by
345	  refine ⟨fun {X Y} f hf ↦ ?_⟩
346	  have hf' : Function.Injective f := (CategoryTheory.mono_iff_injective f).mp hf
347	  rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
348	  exact Finsupp.mapDomain_injective hf'
349	
350	-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).f` is a
351	-- monomorphism in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
352	-- `MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L251–257 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
353	-- The `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` attribute is
354	-- required because the typeclass-search engine needs to unfold the `dsimp`-normal
355	-- form of `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).f` past structure-literal projection.
356	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
357	instance HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono
358	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
359	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
360	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
361	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
362	    Mono (HModule'_shortComplex k S).f := by
363	  have : Mono ((HModule'_shortComplex k S).f ≫ biprod.snd) := by
364	    dsimp
365	    simp only [biprod.lift_snd]
366	    infer_instance
367	  exact mono_of_mono _ biprod.snd
368	
369	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).g` is an
370	epimorphism in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's
371	`MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L259–261 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
372	The proof is a one-line term-mode body using
373	`ShortComplex.exact_and_epi_g_iff_g_is_cokernel` and the iter-019 lemma
374	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf`'s `isColimitCokernelCofork` accessor. -/
375	instance HModule'_shortComplex_g_epi
376	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
377	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
378	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
379	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
380	    Epi (HModule'_shortComplex k S).g :=
381	  ((HModule'_shortComplex k S).exact_and_epi_g_iff_g_is_cokernel.2
382	    ⟨(HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf k S).isColimitCokernelCofork⟩).2
383	
384	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).Exact`,
385	i.e. the kernel of `g` equals the image of `f` in `Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`.
386	Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.lean` L263–265 with
387	`AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The proof is a one-line term-mode
388	body using `ShortComplex.exact_of_g_is_cokernel` and the iter-019 lemma
389	`HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf`'s `isColimitCokernelCofork` accessor. -/
390	lemma HModule'_shortComplex_exact
391	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
392	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
393	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
394	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
395	    (HModule'_shortComplex k S).Exact :=
396	  ShortComplex.exact_of_g_is_cokernel _
397	    (HModule'_isPushoutModuleCatFreeSheaf k S).isColimitCokernelCofork
398	
399	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-020): `(HModule'_shortComplex k S).ShortExact`,
400	the short-exact predicate (combining `Mono f`, `Epi g`, and `Exact`) in
401	`Sheaf J (ModuleCat k)`. Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.lean`
402	L267–268 with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`. The proof is a one-line
403	anonymous-constructor: the `Mono f` and `Epi g` predicates are typeclass-resolved
404	from `HModule'_shortComplex_f_mono` and `HModule'_shortComplex_g_epi`, leaving
405	only the `exact` field which is filled with `HModule'_shortComplex_exact`. The
406	named lemma is consumed in iter-021+ as `S.HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact.extClass`
407	to define the connecting hom `HModule'_δ` of the Mayer-Vietoris LES. -/
408	lemma HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact
409	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
410	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
411	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
412	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) :
413	    (HModule'_shortComplex k S).ShortExact where
414	  exact := HModule'_shortComplex_exact k S

416	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-021): the connecting homomorphism `δ` of the
417	Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology.
418	For a Mayer-Vietoris square `S` and adjacent cohomological degrees `n₀ + 1 = n₁`,
419	the morphism
420	  δ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₀).obj (op S.X₁) ⟶
421	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₁).obj (op S.X₄)
422	in `AddCommGrpCat` is the precomposition with the extension class
423	`(HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S).extClass : Ext (...X₁...) (...X₃...) 1`
424	followed by `AddCommGrpCat.ofHom` to wrap the resulting `AddMonoidHom` as a
425	categorical morphism. Direct mirror of Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.δ`
426	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` L112–114)
427	for the `ModuleCat k` flavor with `AddCommGrpCat.free → ModuleCat.free k`.
428	
429	The `[HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]` typeclass is required (the
430	`Ext.precomp` operation depends on it) and matches the corresponding
431	requirement on iter-016 `HModule'_cohomologyPresheafFunctor` / `..._cohomologyPresheaf`,
432	iter-017 `HModule'_toBiprod` / `..._fromBiprod`, and iter-018
433	`HModule'_toBiprod_fromBiprod`.
434	
435	This connecting hom is the missing third link of the Mayer-Vietoris exact
436	sequence: combined with iter-017's `toBiprod` (sum of restriction) and
437	`fromBiprod` (difference of restriction), iter-018's `toBiprod ≫ fromBiprod = 0`,
438	and the iter-022+ packaging into a `ComposableArrows`-form sequence + iter-023+
439	sequence-iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence` + iter-024+ exactness theorem, it
440	will yield the full LES `... → Hⁿ(X₄) → Hⁿ(X₂) ⊞ Hⁿ(X₃) → Hⁿ(X₁) → Hⁿ⁺¹(X₄) → ...`
441	in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology. -/
442	noncomputable def HModule'_δ
443	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
444	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
445	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
446	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
447	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
448	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
449	    (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₀).obj (Opposite.op S.X₁) ⟶
450	      (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n₁).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄) :=
451	  AddCommGrpCat.ofHom ((HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S).extClass.precomp _ (by omega))
452	
453	/-- Phase A step 6 *Path 2* (iter-022): the Mayer-Vietoris long exact sequence
454	in `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology, packaged as a `ComposableArrows` of
455	length 5 in `AddCommGrpCat`. The five composable morphisms are, in order,
456	`HModule'_toBiprod` at degree `n₀`, `HModule'_fromBiprod` at degree `n₀`, the
457	connecting hom `HModule'_δ` from degree `n₀` to degree `n₁`, `HModule'_toBiprod`
458	at degree `n₁`, and `HModule'_fromBiprod` at degree `n₁`. Direct mirror of
459	Mathlib's `MayerVietorisSquare.sequence`
460	(`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Sites/SheafCohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` L120–122)
461	for the `ModuleCat k` flavor.
462	
463	The `noncomputable abbrev` form is load-bearing: downstream `dsimp`-based
464	unfolding (in iter-023+ `HModule'_sequenceIso` and iter-024+
465	`HModule'_sequence_exact`) needs to access `mk₅`'s field-projection simp
466	lemmas through the abbrev. -/
467	noncomputable abbrev HModule'_sequence
468	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
469	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
470	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
471	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
472	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
473	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
474	    ComposableArrows AddCommGrpCat 5 :=
475	  ComposableArrows.mk₅ (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₀) (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₀)
476	    (HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h)
477	    (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₁) (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₁)
478	
479	/-- Iter-023 aux lemma 1: explicit elementwise formula for `HModule'_toBiprod`
480	(mirror Mathlib `MayerVietoris.lean` L48–64). -/
481	lemma HModule'_toBiprod_apply
482	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
483	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
484	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
485	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
486	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
487	    {n : ℕ} (y : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₄)) :
488	    HModule'_toBiprod k S F n y =
489	      (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).inv
490	        ⟨(HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₂₄.op y,
491	          (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₃₄.op y⟩ := by
492	  apply (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.injective
493	  dsimp [HModule'_toBiprod]
494	  ext
495	  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
496	      ← AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_fst_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
497	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_fst, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
498	  · rw [Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply, Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_apply,
499	      ← AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_snd_apply, Iso.hom_inv_id_apply,
500	      ← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, biprod.lift_snd, Iso.inv_hom_id_apply]
501	
502	/-- Iter-023 aux lemma 2: explicit elementwise formula for `HModule'_fromBiprod`
503	on the inverse of `biprodIsoProd` (mirror Mathlib L77–83). -/
504	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply
505	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
506	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
507	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
508	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
509	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
510	    {n : ℕ}
511	    (y₁ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂))
512	    (y₂ : (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃)) :
513	    HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n

516	        (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).map S.f₁₃.op y₂ := by
517	  dsimp [HModule'_fromBiprod]

519	  simp [AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd_inv_comp_desc, sub_eq_add_neg]

521	-- Iter-023 aux lemma 3: bridges `AddCommGrpCat`-side `biprodIsoProd` and
522	-- `Ext`-side `Ext.biprodAddEquiv` for the `toBiprod` morphism

526	attribute [local simp] HModule'_toBiprod_apply in

531	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
532	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))

535	        ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).hom (HModule'_toBiprod k S F n x)) =

539	-- Iter-023 aux lemma 4: bridges the same machinery for the `fromBiprod`

541	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
542	attribute [local simp] sub_eq_add_neg in
543	lemma HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom
544	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
545	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
546	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
547	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
548	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
549	    {n : ℕ}
550	    (x : ↑((HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₂) ⊞
551	           (HModule'_cohomologyPresheaf k F n).obj (Opposite.op S.X₃))) :
552	    (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (HModule'_shortComplex k S).f).comp

555	      (HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n x) := by

557	    (AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv.symm.surjective x

560	    Iso.addCommGroupIsoToAddEquiv_symm_apply,
561	    HModule'_fromBiprod_biprodIsoProd_inv_apply]
562	  cat_disch

564	-- Iter-023 main: comparison iso from the iter-022 LES sequence to
565	-- `Ext.contravariantSequence` (mirror Mathlib L124–138). The technical heart
566	-- of the Mayer-Vietoris LES.
567	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
568	noncomputable def HModule'_sequenceIso
569	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
570	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
571	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
572	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
573	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
574	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
575	    HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h ≅
576	      Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence (HModule'_shortComplex_shortExact k S)
577	        F n₀ n₁ (by omega) :=
578	  ComposableArrows.isoMk₅ (Iso.refl _)
579	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
580	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
581	    (Iso.refl _) (Iso.refl _)
582	    ((AddCommGrpCat.biprodIsoProd _ _).trans
583	      (Abelian.Ext.biprodAddEquiv.symm).toAddCommGrpIso)
584	    (Iso.refl _)
585	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
586	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
587	    (by dsimp; rw [Category.comp_id, Category.id_comp]; rfl)
588	    (by ext; apply HModule'_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom_toBiprod_apply)
589	    (by ext; symm; apply HModule'_mk₀_f_comp_biprodAddEquiv_symm_biprodIsoProd_hom)
590	
591	/-- Iter-026: Mayer-Vietoris LES exactness theorem (mirror Mathlib
592	`MayerVietoris.lean` L140–141). The iter-022 sequence is exact via the iter-023
593	comparison iso to `Ext.contravariantSequence`. -/
594	lemma HModule'_sequence_exact
595	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
596	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
597	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
598	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
599	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
600	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
601	    (HModule'_sequence k S F n₀ n₁ h).Exact :=
602	  ComposableArrows.exact_of_iso (HModule'_sequenceIso k S F n₀ n₁ h).symm
603	    (Abelian.Ext.contravariantSequence_exact _ _ _ _ _)
604	
605	/-- Iter-026: `δ ≫ toBiprod = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L143–145). -/
606	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
607	lemma HModule'_δ_toBiprod
608	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
609	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
610	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
611	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
612	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
613	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
614	    HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h ≫ HModule'_toBiprod k S F n₁ = 0 :=
615	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 2
616	
617	/-- Iter-026: `fromBiprod ≫ δ = 0` simp companion (mirror Mathlib L147–149). -/
618	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
619	lemma HModule'_fromBiprod_δ
620	    (k : Type u) [Field k]
621	    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
622	    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
623	    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
624	    (S : J.MayerVietorisSquare) (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))
625	    (n₀ n₁ : ℕ) (h : n₀ + 1 = n₁) :
626	    HModule'_fromBiprod k S F n₀ ≫ HModule'_δ k S F n₀ n₁ h = 0 :=
627	  (HModule'_sequence_exact k S F n₀ n₁ h).zero 1
628	
629	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

8	/-!
9	# Basic-open cover wrappers and the substantive Čech acyclicity theorem
10	
11	This file (iter-063 split, cohort 3) contains the `basicOpenCover` family
12	of definitions and identification lemmas (iter-054), the producer
13	`hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen` (iter-055), the
14	`cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt` lemma, and the
15	substantive theorem `basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`
16	(iter-056 → iter-062). The latter currently carries two labelled substep
17	sorries plus the iter-062 `h_a_fun` scaffolding.
18	
19	This is cohort 3 of the original `MayerVietoris.lean` split:
20	* `MayerVietorisCore.lean`: MV LES core + Mathlib gap-fill.
21	* `MayerVietorisCover.lean`: 2-affine cover MV + cover-totality bridges +
22	  `IsCechAcyclicCover` consumers + `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` carrier.
23	* `BasicOpenCech.lean` (this file): basic-open cover wrappers + the
24	  substantive Čech acyclicity theorem.
25	
26	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex`.
27	-/
28	
29	set_option autoImplicit false
30	
31	universe u v
32	
33	open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry
34	
35	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
36	
37	/-- Iter-054: basic-open cover from a spanning subset of `Γ(C.left, U)`.
38	
39	Given a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, this constructs the family of basic opens
40	`s → Opens` mapping each `f ∈ s` to `C.left.basicOpen f`. When `s` spans the
41	unit ideal of `Γ(C.left, U)` and `U` is affine, this family covers `U`
42	(see `basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top`); each member is itself an affine
43	open (see `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen`).
44	
45	Iter-054 is a thin Mathlib-API wrapper (parameterised by the project's
46	`C : Over (Spec (.of k))` convention) so iter-055+ instantiation work for
47	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` can call the constructor +
48	supremum + affine-membership directly.
49	
50	Mirrors the iter-047 foundational parameterised Čech infrastructure pattern:
51	package thin Mathlib wrappers as named project-local declarations so the
52	substantive multi-iteration work downstream focuses purely on content
53	(Koszul acyclicity + Čech-vs-derived comparison) rather than re-deriving
54	infrastructure at every call site. -/
55	noncomputable def basicOpenCover
56	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
57	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
58	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) :
59	    s → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat :=
60	  fun f => C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U))
61	
62	/-- Iter-054: supremum equality for the basic-open cover from generators
63	spanning the unit ideal of an affine open.
64	
65	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤)`, the basic-open
66	cover `basicOpenCover s` covers `U` exactly: `⨆ i, basicOpenCover s i = U`.
67	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff`
68	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L898).
69	
70	Used by iter-055+ when constructing the `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)`
71	instance: the existential's third conjunct `(⨆ i, 𝒰 i = U)` is exactly what
72	this theorem produces. -/
73	theorem basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top
74	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
75	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
76	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
77	    ⨆ i, basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i = U := by
78	  unfold basicOpenCover
79	  exact hU.iSup_basicOpen_eq_self_iff.mpr hs
80	
81	/-- Iter-054: each member of the basic-open cover of an affine open is itself
82	affine.
83	
84	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)` and `(i : s)`, conclude `IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover s i)`.
85	This is a thin wrapper around Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
86	(`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/AffineScheme.lean` L589).
87	
88	Used by iter-055+ when proving the Koszul acyclicity of the basic-open Čech
89	complex: each finite intersection of basic opens is itself basic (hence affine),
90	which is the inductive step for the Koszul-style alternating-sum argument. -/
91	theorem basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen
92	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
93	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
94	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i : s) :
95	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i) := by
96	  unfold basicOpenCover
97	  exact hU.basicOpen i.1
98	
99	/-- Iter-055: producer for `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover` from existence of basic-open
100	Čech-acyclic cover.
101	
102	Given that for every affine open `U` of `C.left` there exists a spanning subset
103	`s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)` with `Ideal.span s = ⊤` such that the basic-open cover
104	`basicOpenCover s : s → Opens` carries both `IsCechAcyclicCover F (basicOpenCover s)`
105	and `HasCechToHModuleIso F (basicOpenCover s)`, conclude `HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F`.
106	
107	Iter-055 specialises iter-053's existence-bundled cover form to the basic-open cover
108	shape constructed iter-054. Strategic value: factors out the existence-bundling step
109	from iter-056+ substantive work, so the iter-056+ Koszul acyclicity branch and the
110	iter-057+ Čech-vs-derived comparison branch can each prove specifically the basic-open
111	form (which the universal Koszul + comparison arguments naturally produce), and the
112	iter-055 producer then bundles their results into iter-053's carrier shape automatically.
113	
114	Body: destructures the per-affine existence to obtain `(s, hs, hacy, hcomp)`, then
115	bundles them into iter-053's existential cover via iter-054's
116	`basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top` to produce the supremum hypothesis. -/
117	theorem hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen
118	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
119	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
120	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
121	    (h : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}, IsAffineOpen U →
122	        ∃ (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (_ : Ideal.span s = ⊤),
123	          IsCechAcyclicCover F (basicOpenCover s) ∧
124	          HasCechToHModuleIso F (basicOpenCover s)) :
125	    HasAffineCechAcyclicCover F where
126	  exists_cover {U} hU := by
127	    obtain ⟨s, hs, hacy, hcomp⟩ := h hU
128	    exact ⟨s, basicOpenCover s, basicOpenCover_supr_of_span_eq_top hU s hs, hacy, hcomp⟩
129	
130	/-- Iter-055: curve specialisation of `hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen` at
131	`F := toModuleKSheaf C`.
132	
133	Mirrors the iter-039/042/043/048/049/050/051/052/053 `_curve` pattern: a thin
134	dot-notation wrapper that saves call sites in the curve setting (where `F` is the
135	structure sheaf) from re-typing `toModuleKSheaf C` whenever the iter-055 producer
136	is chained through. Used by the iter-057+ packaging step to instantiate
137	`HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)` once iter-056+ supplies the
138	per-affine basic-open Čech-acyclic + comparison-iso evidence. -/
139	theorem hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen_curve
140	    {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
141	    [HasExt.{u} (Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
142	    (h : ∀ {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}, IsAffineOpen U →
143	        ∃ (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (_ : Ideal.span s = ⊤),
144	          IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s) ∧
145	          HasCechToHModuleIso (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)) :
146	    HasAffineCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C) :=
147	  hasAffineCechAcyclicCover_of_basicOpen h
148	
149	/-- Iter-056: pairwise basic-open intersection identification.
150	
151	Given a Set `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)` and indices `i j : s`, the intersection of the
152	two basic-open cover members `basicOpenCover s i` and `basicOpenCover s j`
153	equals the basic open of the product `i.1 * j.1`. Direct iteration of Mathlib's
154	`Scheme.basicOpen_mul`.
155	
156	This is the foundational identification for iter-057+ Čech complex evaluation:
157	the Čech cochain at degree 1 indexes pairs `(i, j) : s × s` and assigns the
158	sheaf evaluated at `basicOpenCover s i ⊓ basicOpenCover s j`. Via this lemma,
159	that intersection is identified with `C.left.basicOpen (i.1 * j.1)`, which by
160	`IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (Mathlib) is the localization
161	`Localization.Away (i.1 * j.1)` of `Γ(C.left, U)`. The Koszul-style
162	alternating-sum exactness on these localizations follows from
163	`exact_of_localized_span` (Mathlib) under `Ideal.span s = ⊤`. -/
164	theorem basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul
165	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
166	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
167	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i j : s) :
168	    basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i ⊓ basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s j =
169	      C.left.basicOpen ((i.1 : Γ(C.left, U)) * j.1) := by
170	  unfold basicOpenCover
171	  exact (Scheme.basicOpen_mul C.left _ _).symm
172	
173	/-- Iter-056: pairwise basic-open intersection on an affine open is itself
174	affine.
175	
176	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`, `(s : Set Γ(C.left, U))`, and indices `i j : s`,
177	the intersection `basicOpenCover s i ⊓ basicOpenCover s j` is itself affine.
178	Thin wrapper combining `basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul` (rewriting the
179	intersection as a basic open of the product) with Mathlib's
180	`IsAffineOpen.basicOpen` (membership preservation under basic-open formation).
181	
182	Generalises iter-054's `basicOpenCover_isAffineOpen` (single-index affine
183	membership) to pairwise intersections. Used by iter-057+ Čech evaluation: each
184	finite intersection of basic opens is itself affine, which is the inductive
185	step for the Koszul alternating-sum argument (Stacks 03F7 / Hartshorne III.4.5
186	/ Eisenbud Commutative Algebra §17 Koszul resolution). -/
187	theorem basicOpenCover_inter_isAffineOpen
188	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
189	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
190	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (i j : s) :
191	    IsAffineOpen (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s i ⊓
192	        basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s j) := by
193	  unfold basicOpenCover
194	  rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]
195	  exact hU.basicOpen _
196	
197	/-- Iter-057: n-ary basic-open intersection identification (Finset.inf' form).
198	
199	Given a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`, the infimum (over `t`) of the basic-open
200	cover members `basicOpenCover s i` for `i ∈ t` equals the basic open of the
201	finite product `∏ i ∈ t, i.1` in `Γ(C.left, U)`. Generalises iter-056's binary
202	`basicOpenCover_inter_eq_basicOpen_mul` to arbitrary non-empty Finset indexing.
203	
204	Body via `Finset.cons_induction`: the empty case is impossible by `t.Nonempty`
205	(closed via `Finset.not_nonempty_empty`); the cons case splits on whether the
206	residual `t` is empty or non-empty (via `Finset.eq_empty_or_nonempty`). In the
207	empty residual case, `simp [basicOpenCover]` closes via single-element reduction
208	of `Finset.inf'` and `Finset.prod`. In the non-empty residual case, decompose
209	via `Finset.inf'_cons hne` to expose `basicOpenCover s a ⊓ rest`, decompose
210	`Finset.prod_cons` to expose `a.1 * (∏ i ∈ t, i.1)`, apply the IH to identify
211	`rest = C.left.basicOpen (∏ i ∈ t, i.1)`, then `change ... ⊓ _ = _` followed by
212	`rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]` consolidates the product form.
213	
214	This is the foundational identification for iter-058+ Čech complex evaluation
215	at arbitrary degree `n`: the Čech cochain at degree `n` indexes `x : Fin (n+1) → s`
216	and assigns the sheaf evaluated at `⨅ i : Fin (n+1), basicOpenCover s (x i)`.
217	Via this lemma (instantiated at `t := (Finset.univ : Finset (Fin (n+1))).image x`,
218	or more directly via `Finset.univ.inf' Finset.univ_nonempty (basicOpenCover s ∘ x)`),
219	that intersection identifies with `C.left.basicOpen (∏ i : Fin (n+1), (x i).1)`,
220	which by `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` (Mathlib) is the
221	localization `Localization.Away (∏ i, (x i).1)` of `Γ(C.left, U)`. -/
222	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod
223	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
224	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
225	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
226	    t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) =
227	      C.left.basicOpen (∏ i ∈ t, (i.1 : Γ(C.left, U))) := by
228	  classical
229	  induction t using Finset.cons_induction with
230	  | empty => exact absurd h Finset.not_nonempty_empty
231	  | cons a t ha ih =>
232	    rcases t.eq_empty_or_nonempty with rfl | hne
233	    · simp [basicOpenCover]
234	    · rw [Finset.inf'_cons hne, Finset.prod_cons, ih hne]
235	      change C.left.basicOpen (a.1 : Γ(C.left, U)) ⊓ _ = _
236	      rw [← Scheme.basicOpen_mul]
237	
238	/-- Iter-057: n-ary basic-open intersection on an affine open is itself affine.
239	
240	Given `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`, `(s : Set Γ(C.left, U))`, `(t : Finset s)`, and
241	`(h : t.Nonempty)`, the n-ary intersection `t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)` is
242	itself affine. Term-mode body chains
243	`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod` (rewriting the n-ary intersection
244	as a basic open of the finite product) with Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.basicOpen`
245	(membership preservation under basic-open formation).
246	
247	Generalises iter-056's `basicOpenCover_inter_isAffineOpen` (pairwise affine
248	membership) to arbitrary non-empty Finset indexing. Used by iter-058+ Čech
249	evaluation: each finite intersection of basic opens of an affine is itself
250	affine, which is the inductive step for the Koszul alternating-sum argument
251	(Stacks 03F7 / Hartshorne III.4.5 / Eisenbud Commutative Algebra §17 Koszul
252	resolution).
253	
254	**Body uses `▸` (Eq.mpr), NOT `rw`**: `rw` on the equation produced by Theorem
255	(1) fails because of motive-occurrence issues with the implicit `Subtype.val`
256	coercions on elements of `Finset s`; term-mode `▸` rewrite resolves these
257	directly without invoking the rewriter's pattern-matching. Plan-agent
258	`lean_run_code` probe (this pass) confirmed the term-mode form compiles
259	kernel-only while the tactic-mode `rw` form fails with "Did not find an
260	occurrence of the pattern" error. -/
261	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen
262	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
263	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
264	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
265	    IsAffineOpen (t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) :=
266	  basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod s t h ▸ hU.basicOpen _
267	
268	/-- Iter-058: n-ary basic-open intersection is contained in `U`.
269	
270	Given a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`, the infimum (over `t`) of the basic-open
271	cover members `basicOpenCover s i` for `i ∈ t` is contained in `U`. Term-mode
272	body chains iter-057's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod` (rewriting
273	the n-ary intersection as a single basic open of the finite product) with
274	Mathlib's `Scheme.basicOpen_le` (`X.basicOpen f ≤ U` for any `f ∈ Γ(X, U)`)
275	via Lean's `▸` (Eq.mpr) rewrite.
276	
277	**No `IsAffineOpen` hypothesis required**: `basicOpen_le` holds for any open `U`
278	(the basic open of `f ∈ Γ(X, U)` is always a sub-open of `U`); compare iter-057's
279	`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isAffineOpen` which does need `(hU : IsAffineOpen U)`.
280	
281	This is the inclusion morphism iter-059+ Čech-evaluation arguments need when
282	applying Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen`:
283	```
284	(hU : IsAffineOpen U) (f : Γ(X, U)) {V : X.Opens} (i : V ⟶ U) :
285	  V = X.basicOpen f → IsLocalization.Away f Γ(X, V)
286	```
287	The morphism `i : V ⟶ U` argument (which is `homOfLE` of `V ≤ U`) is needed to
288	install the `Algebra Γ(X, U) Γ(X, V)` instance for the conclusion type, since
289	Mathlib's `algebra_section_section_basicOpen` instance only fires for the
290	syntactic form `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`, not for `V = t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)`.
291	Iter-058's inclusion lemma supplies this `V ≤ U` proof so iter-059+ can call
292	`hU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen f (homOfLE (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le ...))
293	(basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod ...)` to land
294	`IsLocalization.Away (∏ i ∈ t, i.1) Γ(C.left, t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s))`
295	directly. -/
296	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le
297	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
298	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
299	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
300	    t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) ≤ U :=
301	  basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod s t h ▸
302	    C.left.basicOpen_le _
303	
304	/-- Iter-059: n-ary basic-open intersection is a localization.
305	
306	Given an affine open `U`, a subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`, and a non-empty `Finset t : Finset s`,
307	the structure sheaf evaluated on the n-ary intersection of basic-open cover members
308	`t.inf' h (basicOpenCover s)` is identified — as a localization away from the product
309	`∏ i ∈ t, i.1` of generators — with `Γ(C.left, U)`. The algebra structure on the conclusion
310	type is given explicitly by `(C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE _).op).hom.toAlgebra`, threaded
311	through the conclusion `@IsLocalization.Away _ _ f Γ(X, V) _ algebra` form (matching
312	Mathlib's `IsAffineOpen.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen` signature exactly).
313	
314	**Explicit `@`-algebra binding is essential.** Mathlib's default
315	`algebra_section_section_basicOpen` instance (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Scheme.lean` L724)
316	fires only for the syntactic form `Γ(X, X.basicOpen f)`, *not* for arbitrary `V`
317	definitionally equal to `X.basicOpen f`. The iter-058 plan-agent probe-confirmed that
318	stating this theorem with *inferred* algebra fails with
319	`failed to synthesize instance of type class Algebra Γ(C.left, U) Γ(C.left, t.inf' h …)`.
320	Threading the algebra structure as part of the conclusion type — matching Mathlib's
321	signature exactly — sidesteps the synthesis attempt.
322	
323	This is the **fourth and final** thin foundational scaffolding helper on the affine
324	basic-open identification side. The iter-060+ basic-open Koszul acyclicity argument
325	(per the iter-059 analogy report at `analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`, Branch A
326	substep 2) chains iter-057's equality + iter-058's inclusion + iter-059's localization
327	+ `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean`)
328	to identify each Čech-cochain factor `F(⋂_k 𝒰(x k))` with the cover-of-`D(f)` Čech complex
329	on localised modules. -/
330	theorem basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization
331	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
332	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
333	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (t : Finset s) (h : t.Nonempty) :
334	    @IsLocalization.Away _ _ (∏ i ∈ t, (i.1 : Γ(C.left, U)))
335	      Γ(C.left, t.inf' h (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s)) _
336	      ((C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE
337	        (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le (C := C) (U := U) s t h)).op).hom.toAlgebra) :=
338	  hU.isLocalization_of_eq_basicOpen _
339	    (homOfLE (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le (C := C) (U := U) s t h))
340	    (basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod (C := C) (U := U) s t h)
341	
342	/-- Iter-066 helper: in `ModuleCat k`, a product projection induced by an
343	injection of index sets is a split epi.
344	
345	Given an injection `f : β → α` and a family `M : α → ModuleCat k`, the natural
346	projection `∏ᶜ_a M a ⟶ ∏ᶜ_b M (f b)` defined as `Pi.lift (fun b => Pi.π M (f b))`
347	(which sends a tuple indexed by `α` to its `image f` components, reindexed by `β`)
348	admits a section in `ModuleCat k`. The section is the "extend by zero" map:
349	given a tuple indexed by `β`, fill in the components in `image f` (using the
350	unique preimage given by injectivity of `f`) and set the remaining components
351	to `0`.
352	
353	This is the categorical fact underlying `h_π_split` in
354	`basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf`: the cochain-complex map
355	`π : K ⟶ K₀` is at each degree of this form (the `↑s₀ ↪ s` inclusion induces a
356	postcomposition map `(Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀) → (Fin (i+1) → s)` which is injective by
357	injectivity of the inclusion, hence the induced product projection on cochain
358	factors is split).
359	
360	The helper is stated for a generic injection so it can be reused for both the
361	`s` substep (`h_π_split` proper) and the slice-cover variants. -/
362	noncomputable def splitEpi_pi_lift_of_injective {k : Type u} [Field k]
363	    {α β : Type u} (M : α → ModuleCat.{u} k)
364	    (f : β → α) (hf : Function.Injective f) :
365	    SplitEpi (Pi.lift (fun b : β => Pi.π M (f b))) where
366	  section_ := by
367	    classical
368	    refine Pi.lift (fun a => ?_)
369	    by_cases h : ∃ b, f b = a
370	    · exact h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π (fun b => M (f b)) h.choose
371	    · exact 0
372	  id := by
373	    classical
374	    apply Pi.hom_ext
375	    intro b
376	    rw [Category.assoc, Pi.lift_π, Category.id_comp, Pi.lift_π]
377	    have h : ∃ b', f b' = f b := ⟨b, rfl⟩
378	    rw [dif_pos h]
379	    -- The transport `h.choose_spec ▸ Pi.π _ h.choose = Pi.π _ b`.
380	    -- By injectivity of `f`, `h.choose = b`, so the transport collapses.
381	    -- We use the helper lemma below.
382	    have key : ∀ {b b' : β} (heq : f b' = f b),
383	        heq ▸ (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b' :
384	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b')) =
385	          (Pi.π (fun b'' => M (f b'')) b :
386	            (∏ᶜ fun b'' => M (f b'')) ⟶ M (f b)) := by
387	      intro b b' heq
388	      have hbb : b' = b := hf heq
389	      subst hbb
390	      rfl
391	    exact key h.choose_spec
392	
393	/-- Iter-060 helper: bridge from `ExactAt n` on the Čech cochain complex to
394	`Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)`.
395	
396	The chain is two steps: `HomologicalComplex.ExactAt.isZero_homology` packages an
397	`ExactAt n` hypothesis into `IsZero ((cechCochain ...).homology n)`, and
398	`ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero` then upgrades `IsZero` on a `ModuleCat`
399	object to `Subsingleton` on its underlying carrier type.
400	
401	This is the structural skeleton common to *every* `IsCechAcyclicCover` instance
402	proof: the actual mathematical content lives in establishing
403	`(cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n` for each `n > 0`. -/
404	theorem cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt
405	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
406	    {F : Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology C.left.toTopCat) (ModuleCat.{u} k)}
407	    {ι : Type u} {𝒰 : ι → TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat}
408	    {n : ℕ} (h : (cechCochain C F 𝒰).ExactAt n) :
409	    Subsingleton (cechCohomology C F 𝒰 n) :=
410	  ModuleCat.subsingleton_of_isZero h.isZero_homology
411	
412	/-- Iter-060 (Phase A step 6 / Path 2 — **substantive Čech acyclicity attempt**):
413	the basic-open cover of an affine open `U` by a spanning subset `s ⊆ Γ(C.left, U)`
414	gives a Čech-acyclic cover for the `ModuleCat k`-valued structure sheaf
415	`toModuleKSheaf C`. This is the Stacks 01ED Čech-acyclicity theorem for affine
416	schemes in our setting.
417	
418	**Mathematical content (Stacks 01ED route).** For each positive degree `n`,
419	exactness of the Čech cochain at degree `n` is a *local* property on the
420	spanning set `s`. Localising the Čech complex at any `f ∈ s` exhibits the
421	slice over `D(f)` as a Čech complex of an affine cover whose terminal vertex
422	`D(f) → D(f)` supplies an *extra degeneracy* (Mathlib's
423	`FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`). By
424	`SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` the augmented
425	chain complex of the slice is homotopy equivalent to the constant complex on
426	the augmentation, hence exact in positive degrees. Re-globalising via
427	`exact_of_isLocalized_span` (Mathlib `RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean`
428	L173) yields exactness of the *unlocalised* Čech complex at degree `n`.
429	
430	The structural skeleton is:
431	* reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to `ExactAt (cechCochain) n` via
432	  `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`;
433	* the substantive `ExactAt n` is decomposed into three substeps tracked by
434	  separate `sorry`s, each annotated with the missing computational step.
435	
436	**Transient sorries (iter-060)** — explicitly endorsed by the iteration
437	directive; each sorry names a substep. The substeps are:
438	  (a) **local exactness at `D(f)`** (extra-degeneracy invocation): given the
439	      cover-of-`D(f)` slice, build the augmented Čech simplicial object,
440	      apply `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` to obtain an
441	      `ExtraDegeneracy`, then transport via
442	      `ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv` and op-passage `CochainComplex.opEquivalence`
443	      to get cochain-level exactness in positive degrees;
444	  (b) **localised-Čech identification**: the localisation at `f` of the
445	      degree-`n` Čech cochain is the degree-`n` cochain of the cover-of-`D(f)`
446	      Čech complex, via iter-057+ identifications chained with
447	      `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`;
448	  (c) **local-to-global** via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`: the assembled
449	      family of per-`f` local exactnesses lifts to global exactness.
450	
451	The decomposition follows §"Branch A — Čech acyclicity" of
452	`analogies/cech-koszul-precedent.md`. -/
453	theorem basicOpenCover_isCechAcyclicCover_toModuleKSheaf
454	    {k : Type u} [Field k] {C : Over (Spec (.of k))}
455	    {U : TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat} (hU : IsAffineOpen U)
456	    (s : Set Γ(C.left, U)) (hs : Ideal.span s = ⊤) :
457	    IsCechAcyclicCover (toModuleKSheaf C)
458	        (basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s) where
459	  subsingleton_cechCohomology n hn := by
460	    -- Step 0 (structural): reduce `Subsingleton (cechCohomology n)` to
461	    -- `ExactAt n` of the underlying Čech cochain complex via the iter-060
462	    -- helper `cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt`.
463	    refine cechCohomology_subsingleton_of_cechCochain_exactAt (n := n) ?_
464	    -- The goal is now
465	    --   `(cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)).ExactAt n`.
466	    -- Iter-061 decomposition: the Stacks 01ED extra-degeneracy + local-to-global
467	    -- argument has three substeps tracked by separate labelled `sorry`s.
468	    --
469	    -- ===========================================================================
470	    -- Substep (a) — Extra-degeneracy on the slice cover of `D(f)`.
471	    -- ===========================================================================
472	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover of `D(f)` by the family
473	    --   `{basicOpenCover s f' ⊓ D(f)}_{f' ∈ s}`
474	    -- contains `D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)` itself (at index `f' = f`), so the
475	    -- terminal vertex maps to `D(f)` via the identity section
476	    -- `D(f) → D(f)`. By `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech`
477	    -- (`Mathlib/CategoryTheory/Limits/FormalCoproducts/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L92),
478	    -- the augmented Čech simplicial object on this slice has an
479	    -- `ExtraDegeneracy`. By `SimplicialObject.Augmented.ExtraDegeneracy.homotopyEquiv`
480	    -- (`Mathlib/AlgebraicTopology/ExtraDegeneracy.lean` L328), the associated
481	    -- alternating-face-map chain complex is homotopy-equivalent to the
482	    -- constant complex on the augmentation. Op-passage through
483	    -- `CochainComplex.opEquivalence` then yields cochain-level exactness in
484	    -- positive degrees. We state substep (a) as a concrete `ExactAt n`
485	    -- claim on the slice cover Čech cochain complex:
486	    have h_a : ∀ (f : s),
487	        (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
488	            (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
489	              C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))).ExactAt n := by
490	      intro f
491	      -- The terminal vertex of the slice cover is at `f' = f`:
492	      --   `basicOpenCover s f ⊓ C.left.basicOpen f.1 = D(f) ⊓ D(f) = D(f)`.
493	      -- Plug into `FormalCoproduct.extraDegeneracyCech` with `i₀ := f`,
494	      -- extract `homotopyEquiv`, op-pass, read off `ExactAt n` for `0 < n`.
495	      sorry  -- substep (a) infrastructure: augmented Čech simplicial object
496	             -- + extraDegeneracyCech + homotopyEquiv + CochainComplex.opEquivalence
497	    -- ===========================================================================
498	    -- Substep (b) — Localised-Čech identification.
499	    -- ===========================================================================
500	    -- For each `f ∈ s`, the slice cover Čech cochain complex above is
501	    -- isomorphic (in `ModuleCat k`) to the localisation at `f` of the
502	    -- original Čech cochain complex `K`. Cochain-factor-wise:
503	    --   `(slice cover).X^n = ∏_x F(⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k) ⊓ D(f))`
504	    --                      = `∏_x F(D(f · ∏ k, (x k).1))`              (Stacks identity)
505	    --                      = `∏_x Localization.Away (f · ∏ k, (x k).1) Γ(C.left, U)`
506	    --                      = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⨅ k, basicOpenCover s (x k))[1/f]`
507	    --                      = `K.X^n [1/f]`.
508	    -- The first equality is iter-057 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_eq_basicOpen_prod`)
509	    -- applied to the augmented index `{f} ∪ image(x)`. The second is
510	    -- iter-059 (`basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_isLocalization`). The third is
511	    -- the universal property of localisation (transitivity of localisation:
512	    -- `R[1/(f·g)] = R[1/g][1/f]` when `f` is also among the generators).
513	    -- The differential identification uses `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`
514	    -- (`Mathlib/Algebra/Module/LocalizedModule/Exact.lean` L56) to transport
515	    -- the cochain-level identification to the slice differential.
516	    --
517	    -- ===========================================================================
518	    -- Substep (c) — Local-to-global via `exact_of_isLocalized_span`.
519	    -- ===========================================================================
520	    -- The combined data from substeps (a) and (b) — per-`f` exactness at
521	    -- degree `n` of the slice cover Čech complex, which by substep (b) is
522	    -- the localisation of `K.sc n` — fits the hypothesis of
523	    -- `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ...`
524	    -- (`Mathlib/RingTheory/LocalProperties/Exactness.lean` L173) viewing
525	    -- the cochain factors as `Γ(C.left, U)`-modules through their natural
526	    -- module structure. The conclusion is `Function.Exact ⇑(K.sc n).f ⇑(K.sc n).g`,
527	    -- which converts back to `K.ExactAt n` via
528	    -- `ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact` and
529	    -- `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff`.
530	    --
531	    -- This combined assembly remains as a single transient sorry: it
532	    -- requires constructing the cochain-level localisation iso (substep (b)
533	    -- ≈ a `noncomputable def` for the localised Čech short complex plus
534	    -- its identification iso) and routing the ring-base mismatch
535	    -- (`(K.sc n).f.hom` is `k`-linear; `exact_of_isLocalized_span` consumes
536	    -- `Γ(C.left, U)`-linear maps — they coincide as set-functions, but the
537	    -- type-level reconciliation is non-trivial).
538	    --
539	    -- For iter-061, substep (a)'s claim is concretely stated above and
540	    -- substep (b)+(c)'s assembly remains as the second labelled sorry below.
541	    -- We perform the mechanical conversion `K.ExactAt n ↔ Function.Exact ⇑f ⇑g`
542	    -- before the sorry, so the remaining content is precisely the
543	    -- "transport per-f exactness through the localisation iso then apply
544	    -- the local-to-global principle" assembly step.
545	    rw [HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff,
546	        ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact]
547	    -- Goal:
548	    --   `Function.Exact ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((K).sc n).f)
549	    --                   ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((K).sc n).g)`
550	    -- where `K := cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C) (basicOpenCover s)`.
551	    -- ===========================================================================
552	    -- Iter-062 substantive scaffolding (a): convert `h_a` into `Function.Exact` form.
553	    -- ===========================================================================
554	    -- The same `HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff` +
555	    -- `ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact` chain that
556	    -- converts our goal also converts each per-`f` slice-cover `ExactAt n`
557	    -- hypothesis into a per-`f` `Function.Exact` statement at the
558	    -- function-level. This is closed under `← rw` (no new sorry).
559	    have h_a_fun : ∀ (f : s),
560	        Function.Exact
561	          ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc
562	              (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
563	                (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
564	                  C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))) n).f)
565	          ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom (HomologicalComplex.sc
566	              (cechCochain C (toModuleKSheaf C)
567	                (fun (f' : s) => basicOpenCover (C := C) (U := U) s f' ⊓
568	                  C.left.basicOpen (f : Γ(C.left, U)))) n).g) := by
569	      intro f
570	      rw [← ShortComplex.ShortExact.moduleCat_exact_iff_function_exact,
571	          ← HomologicalComplex.exactAt_iff]
572	      exact h_a f
573	    -- ===========================================================================
574	    -- Iter-062 substantive scaffolding (b): outline the local-to-global route.
575	    -- ===========================================================================
576	    -- The closure of the remaining goal proceeds via Mathlib's local-to-global
577	    -- principle for exactness `exact_of_isLocalized_span` applied to the
578	    -- ring `R := Γ(C.left, U)`, the spanning subset `s ⊆ R`, and the
579	    -- `R`-linear maps `(K.sc n).f.hom` and `(K.sc n).g.hom` (treated as
580	    -- `R`-linear via the `Module R ((K.sc n).X i)`-structure obtained from
581	    -- the cochain factor's natural `Γ(C.left, ⋂_x …)`-module structure
582	    -- precomposed with the algebra map `R → Γ(C.left, ⋂_x …)`).
583	    -- The required input is, for each `f ∈ s`, a `Function.Exact`
584	    -- statement on the localised cochain factors at `f`. By the
585	    -- cochain-factor identification chain (iter-057 + iter-058 + iter-059
586	    -- + Mathlib `Localization.localizationLocalization` + product-localisation
587	    -- commutation), the localised `(K.sc n).X i [1/f]` identifies with the
588	    -- slice-cover Čech `.X i` at the same degree, and consequently the
589	    -- localised differential identifies with the slice-cover differential.
590	    -- This identification + `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact` transports
591	    -- `h_a_fun f` (which is the slice-cover Čech `Function.Exact` at
592	    -- degree `n`) to `Function.Exact` of the localised differential.
593	    -- The infinite-set subtlety (`s` is `Set`, not `Finset`) is sidestepped
594	    -- because `Ideal.span s = ⊤` already implies a *finite* subset spans
595	    -- the unit ideal — `exact_of_isLocalized_span` only consumes the per-`r`
596	    -- localised exactness data and the spanning hypothesis, not finiteness
597	    -- of `s` directly.
598	    -- ===========================================================================
599	    -- Substep (b1) — install the `Module Γ(C.left, U)` instances on each
600	    -- cochain factor `(K.sc n).X i`.
601	    -- ===========================================================================
602	    -- The cochain factor `K.X i = ∏_{x : Fin (i+1) → s} Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k))`
603	    -- carries a `Γ(C.left, U)`-module structure pointwise, since each
604	    -- `Γ(C.left, V)` for `V ≤ U` is a `Γ(C.left, U)`-algebra via the
605	    -- restriction map (iter-058's `basicOpenCover_finset_inf'_le` supplies
606	    -- the inclusion `V ≤ U`, iter-006's `algebraSection` supplies the
607	    -- algebra structure on each factor).
608	    --
609	    -- ===========================================================================
610	    -- Substep (b2) — exhibit `slice_K.X i` as `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1`
611	    -- of `(K.sc n).X i` for each `f ∈ s`.
612	    -- ===========================================================================
613	    -- The cochain-factor identification chain (iter-057 + iter-058 + iter-059
614	    -- + Mathlib `Localization.localizationLocalization` + product-localisation
615	    -- commutation) gives
616	    --   `slice_K.X i = ∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰_slice(x k))`
617	    --                = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k) ⊓ D(f))`
618	    --                = `∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k))[1/f]`        (iter-058 + iter-059)
619	    --                = `(∏_x Γ(C.left, ⋂_k 𝒰(x k)))[1/f]`     (product-loc commutation)
620	    --                = `(K.X i)[1/f]`.
621	    -- The pointwise localisation map (component-wise restriction map
622	    -- `Γ(C.left, V) → Γ(C.left, V')` for `V' = V ⊓ D(f) ≤ V`) is the canonical
623	    -- localisation map and an `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` instance.
624	    --
625	    -- ===========================================================================
626	    -- Substep (c) — apply `exact_of_isLocalized_span s hs ... h_a_fun`.
627	    -- ===========================================================================
628	    -- With the data from (b1) and (b2), `exact_of_isLocalized_span` packages
629	    -- the per-`f` exactness `h_a_fun f` into the global `Function.Exact`
630	    -- conclusion. The remaining work is purely the unification of the per-`f`
631	    -- input shape with the conclusion of `IsLocalizedModule.map ... F`
632	    -- via the iso from (b2) and `IsLocalizedModule.map_exact`.
633	    -- ===========================================================================
634	    -- Iter-063 progress: explicit finite-subspanning extraction + obstruction
635	    -- diagnosis.
636	    -- ===========================================================================
637	    -- The localisation-commutes-with-products subtlety identified by the
638	    -- iter-061 prover (Attempt 3) is genuine and severe: for the cochain
639	    -- factor `K.X i = ∏ᶜ_{x : Fin (i+1) → s} P.obj (op (∏ᶜ_k 𝒰(x k)))` in
640	    -- `ModuleCat k` (where `P` is the underlying presheaf of `toModuleKSheaf C`),
641	    -- the index set `Fin (i+1) → s` is *infinite* when `s` is infinite, and
642	    -- `LocalizedModule (powers f) (∏ᶜ_x M_x) ≄ ∏ᶜ_x LocalizedModule (powers f) M_x`
643	    -- in general for infinite products. So `slice_K.X i = ∏_x Γ(V_x ⊓ D(f))`
644	    -- is NOT literally `(K.X i)[1/f]` — the natural map between them is
645	    -- *not* an `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1`. Iter-062's substep (b2) plan
646	    -- ("install `IsLocalizedModule.Away f.1` instances on the product")
647	    -- requires reducing to a finite index-set first.
648	    --
649	    -- Iter-063 lands the finite-subspanning extraction as a structural fact
650	    -- inside the proof: `Ideal.span s = ⊤` implies the existence of a
651	    -- *finite* subset `s₀ ⊆ s` with `Ideal.span ↑s₀ = ⊤` via Mathlib's
652	    -- `Ideal.span_eq_top_iff_finite`. The remaining substep work uses `s₀`
653	    -- (a `Finset`, hence `Fin (i+1) → ↑s₀` is finite) to install the
654	    -- product-localisation commutation. The transport step from `s₀`-cover
655	    -- exactness to `s`-cover exactness is itself non-trivial: it requires
656	    -- a Čech-complex refinement comparison (`s₀ ⊆ s` ⇒ Čech-cohomology
657	    -- equality), which Mathlib does not currently expose directly but can
658	    -- be derived from the universal property of products via `Pi.eqOfPi`

976	    -- Close the original goal via the transport `K₀ exact → K exact` + exactness of `K₀`.
977	    -- ===========================================================================
978	    exact h_transport h_K₀_exact
979	
980	
981	end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme
982
